docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:26:- **Decision generated:** `ddx agent execute-bead` checks out an isolated git worktree at a specific base revision, runs the agent with only the bead description and governing artifacts, then merges or preserves the result. Failed attempts re-queue with a fresh worktree, not a resumed session.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:29:- **DDx feature it shapes:** `ddx agent execute-bead` and `ddx work` (the queue drainer) — both create a new worktree per attempt and pass review findings forward only as explicit `<review-findings>` prompt sections, not as session state.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:34:- **Decision generated:** Each `execute-bead` invocation writes an execution bundle (timestamp + short hash) containing the prompt, agent transcript, and either the merge result or a `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Bundles are committed.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:37:- **DDx feature it shapes:** `.ddx/executions/<timestamp>-<hash>/` bundles produced by `ddx agent execute-bead`, including the `no_changes_rationale.txt` convention for intentional non-commits.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:50:- **Decision generated:** `ddx agent execute-bead` produces a merge commit (or preserved branch ref) rather than rebasing or squashing. Failed iterations are preserved as refs, not discarded. Worktrees are isolated so a bad run touches nothing in the user's main checkout.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:51:- **Alternative rejected:** Squash or filter execute-bead history to keep `git log` clean, and auto-discard timed-out iteration refs. Rejected because it destroys evidence and forecloses on manual conflict resolution when an agent's work is salvageable.
docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:53:- **DDx feature it shapes:** The execute-bead merge policy — plain fast-forward or `--no-ff` merge commit only, never squash/rebase/filter — and the preservation of timed-out iteration branches as named refs for manual triage.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-f7f09b6e/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead: ddx-f7f09b6e — Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through (ADR-004 step 2)

Result: no changes needed; AC already satisfied by prior commits.

Audit against acceptance criteria
---------------------------------

AC1. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl is recognized as a valid bead collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/archive.go:11 declares
    BeadsArchiveCollection = "beads-archive".
  - cli/internal/bead/registry.go:126-130 registers the spec in the default
    registry with JSONLFile "beads-archive.jsonl" and LockDirName
    "beads-archive.lock".
  - The archive partner store opens via NewStoreWithCollection(s.Dir,
    BeadsArchiveCollection) (archive.go:42), so the JSONL backend resolves
    it to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl by the same registry path.

AC2. ddx bead show <id> finds beads in either active or archive.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:319 wires `bead show` to s.GetWithArchive.
  - Implementation: archive.go:164-177 — first tries the active Get, then
    falls back to the archivePartner. Active-collection guard at line 168
    prevents recursion when the caller is already on the archive collection.
  - Test: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (cli/internal/bead/archive_test.go:150-183) seeds an active and an
    archived bead, runs Archive(), and asserts GetWithArchive resolves both
    IDs and errors on a missing one. PASS.

AC3. ddx bead list has a --include-archive flag (or equivalent) and the
default behavior is documented.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:846 declares the equivalent flag:
    cmd.Flags().Bool("all", false, "Include archived beads
    (.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl)").
  - Default behavior is to consult the archive partner via ListWithArchive
    (bead.go:808-812). The flag is a no-op kept for compatibility, with the
    behavior documented in the inline comment at bead.go:808-810.
  - Implementation: archive.go:182-209 — union with active-wins precedence,
    archive-missing tolerated as empty.
  - Test: TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (archive_test.go:216-245)
    exercises both the active-only List() and the merged ListWithArchive().

AC4. ddx bead ready / blocked still operate only on the active collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1224-1344 — Ready() / ReadyExecution() route
    through readyFiltered() which calls s.ReadAll(), the active-collection
    read. The archive partner is never consulted.
  - cli/internal/bead/store.go:1347-1469 — Blocked() / BlockedAll() also
    call s.ReadAll(); no archive read-through.
  - Test: TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly
    (archive_test.go:185-214) seeds a closed-and-archived bead alongside an
    open active bead, runs Archive() so the closed entry truly leaves the
    active file, then asserts Ready() returns only the open bead and
    Blocked() is empty. PASS.

AC5. ddx work / agent execute-loop ignores archive when picking next work.

  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:199 declares the worker's Store
    interface as ReadyExecution()-only.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1088 — w.Store.ReadyExecution()
    is the queue source, which (per AC4) runs against the active collection
    only.
  - The same readyFiltered active-only ReadAll covers this path; no
    additional plumbing reaches into the archive partner from the loop.

AC6. New tests cover read-through for show, and active-only behavior for
ready/blocked.

  - Read-through for show: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (archive_test.go:150).
  - Active-only behavior for ready/blocked:
    TestArchiveReadyAndBlockedQueryActiveOnly (archive_test.go:185).
  - Supporting coverage: TestArchiveMovesEligibleClosedBeads (line 36),
    TestArchivePreservesReferencedClosedBeads (line 119),
    TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (line 216),
    TestArchiveOpportunisticTriggerOnClose (line 247).

Verification
------------

  cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...   → ok (4.933s)
  cd cli && go test ./cmd/ -run TestBead  → ok (1.617s)

Why nothing was committed
-------------------------

The bead description treats archive read-through as net-new work building on
ddx-2f453147. The earlier slice in this ADR-004 decomposition already shipped
the registry, the archive partner store, GetWithArchive / ListWithArchive,
the `bead show` and `bead list` wiring in cli/cmd/bead.go, the active-only
behavior of Ready/Blocked/ReadyExecution (which falls out of using ReadAll
on the active store), and the matching tests in archive_test.go. There is
no remaining gap to fill for any of the six AC items, so committing
additional code or tests would be churn rather than progress.

If a future review disagrees and identifies a specific AC item that is in
fact unmet, the natural next slice would be to add the missing piece (e.g.
a fully-isolated `--include-archive` flag distinct from the legacy `--all`
no-op, or a dedicated cmd-level integration test for `bead show` against an
archived ID). None of those gaps are visible in the current code.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

## Decision

Stopping without a commit. This bead's acceptance criteria describe a
multi-PR release-adoption epic, not a single bounded change. Attempting it
in one execute-bead pass would produce either (a) a partial commit that
fails most AC items, or (b) a sprawling change touching dispatch, config
schema, CLI surface, frontend Playwright fixtures, and CHANGELOG/docs
without the up-front design alignment the bead itself calls for.

## What is already done (verified in this worktree)

1. **Upstream routing-plan beads are all closed.** All six beads under
   label `routing-plan` in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` are in
   `closed` status:
     - agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware routing)
     - agent-191a74f9 (default + local + standard profiles)
     - agent-1f46cf22 (routing docs)
     - agent-53f38d95 (public decision trace)
     - agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
     - agent-dfabb10b (typed errors)

2. **Their closing commits are in DDx's currently-pinned upstream tag.**
   `cli/go.mod` references `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git tag --contains` for each routing-plan closing commit returns
   v0.9.10 as the earliest containing tag. v0.9.23 (and v0.9.24 latest)
   transitively contain all of them. So the *binary surface* AC #1
   wants is already accessible via the pinned go.mod.

## What is NOT done — and why this can't be a one-shot pass

### AC #1 — CHANGELOG-as-released-narrative

AC #1 asks for "a ddx-agent version whose CHANGELOG documents: first-class
default and local profiles; supported-models allow-list in routing inputs +
gating; ErrHarnessModelIncompatible; public decision-trace …; cost
dimension on ProviderEntry with unknown-cost fallback."

Reading `~/Projects/agent/CHANGELOG.md`:
  - v0.9.10 documents prompt-caching work, not the routing-plan work.
  - v0.9.11 documents ADR-006 override-as-failure-signal.
  - v0.9.12-v0.9.24 cover lucebox/vllm, conformance, corpus, terminal-bench,
    pi-runner panic. None of these releases bundles a coherent
    "routing point release" narrative.

The closing commits for the six beads landed scattered between tags as
side-quest work; none of those tags' release notes call out the
combined contract DDx now wants to consume. AC #1 effectively requires
either (a) an upstream re-release that publishes a "v0.9.25 — routing
point release" entry covering exactly those items, OR (b) DDx's bead
description must be relaxed to accept "the code is in v0.9.23, even if
no single CHANGELOG block summarizes it." Either resolution is a
governance decision, not a coding task this worktree can execute.

### AC #2-7 — DDx-side surface (D1-D5)

These criteria collectively require:
  - rewriting the worker dispatch path so the default issues exactly one
    `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call with no Model / no Harness /
    no Provider, removing all DDx-side ladder synthesis;
  - inserting a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before bead claim
    (`execute_bead_loop.go` ~line 341);
  - making `agent.routing.default_harness` a hard error at config load,
    `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides` opt-in via `--escalate`/`--override-model`,
    with one-time process warnings;
  - guaranteeing override flag combinations route through unchanged to
    upstream and surface upstream typed errors;
  - removing `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef` /
    `workersByHarness` escalation helpers from the default path with
    dead-code detection in CI;
  - adding an integration test that reproduces the historical 19-burn
    drain-queue bug AND new e2e tests covering each override
    combination (claude+opus, model-only, harness-only, profile+harness
    conflict);
  - adding Playwright drain-queue golden fixtures matching the user's
    real config;
  - updating CHANGELOG and docs.

This is on the order of 1-3k LOC delta across:
  - `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go`
  - `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go`
  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` (workerDispatchAdapter)
  - `cli/internal/config/*` (deprecated field schema)
  - `cli/cmd/agent_*.go` (override flag plumbing)
  - new tests (unit, integration, regression, Playwright)

That work spans D1-D5 in the bead's own sequencing list, which the bead
explicitly bundles into "one DDx PR" — but a PR of that size needs
architectural alignment with the user (especially around the breaking
config change in D4 and the dead-code detector in AC #7) before the
first line is written. execute-bead's contract is to satisfy the AC
deterministically, not to make architecture decisions.

### AC #8 — D6 / D7 follow-up beads

The bead requires that follow-up beads be filed:
  - one for the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer,
  - acknowledgement that `ddx-05b4cc9d` (workersByProject filter)
    stays independent.

Filing beads requires `ddx bead create` invocations against the
project's tracker and a commit that mutates `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. This is
mechanical but should ride along with the substantive PR, not a no-op.

### AC #10 — drain-queue regression golden

A net-new integration test that reproduces the user's historical
19-burn failure with their real config. Designing this test
(particularly the gemini-binary-installed assertion, since CI may not
have gemini available) is itself a sub-bead.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

To make this bead landable in a single execute-bead pass, the bead
must be split into smaller beads, each of which is a well-defined PR:

1. **routing-bump** — bump `cli/go.mod` from v0.9.23 to whatever
   tag (v0.9.25?) the upstream cuts as the routing point release; add
   DDx CHANGELOG entry citing upstream release notes. Trivially
   satisfies AC #1 once upstream cuts the tag.

2. **routing-default-path** — D1 + D2 only. Worker dispatch + default
   `ddx agent run` and `ddx work` issue `ResolveRoute(Profile:
   "default")`. New unit tests. Does not touch config schema yet.

3. **routing-preflight-gate** — D3 only. Insert preflight before
   `Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~341`. Test with seeded
   incompatibility.

4. **routing-config-deprecation** — D4 only. Hard error on
   `default_harness`, opt-in warnings on others. Migration test +
   CHANGELOG breaking-change note.

5. **routing-overrides-e2e** — D5 + AC #5 e2e tests for each override
   combination.

6. **routing-drain-regression** — AC #10. Standalone regression test
   that reproduces the historical 19-burn bug.

7. **routing-cleanup** — AC #7 dead-code detector + removal of
   compensating logic.

8. **routing-frontend-goldens** — AC #6 Playwright fixtures.

9. **routing-followups** — AC #8 file the route-status / providers-UI
   bead.

Each of those is a clean execute-bead pass with provable AC. The
current bead's AC list is the union of all nine — too broad for one
attempt.

## Recommendation to the orchestrator

Reopen this bead with `helix-evolve` (or equivalent) to split it into
the nine sub-beads above, then queue them in order. The first three
(bump + default-path + preflight-gate) unblock the user's stated
goals (G1/G2/G3) in the smallest possible PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") suggests
this bead was meant as the EPIC, not the deliverable. Treating it as
the deliverable in execute-bead is what produced this no-op outcome.

rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

This is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt
20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the
upstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a
multi-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be
split before another implementation attempt.

## What is verified done

1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are
   `closed`:
     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,
     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.

2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:
   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the
   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked
   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary
   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no
   `go.mod` bump is even required.

## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit

The bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:

  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default
    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so
    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call —
    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,
    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.
  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before
    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.
  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes
    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`
    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema
    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.
  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests
    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and
    profile+harness conflict.
  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real
    endpoint config + a "no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback"
    assertion.
  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side
    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a
    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of
    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.
  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)
    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.
  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write
    DDx CHANGELOG entry.
  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the
    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —
    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)
    that is itself a sub-bead.

Conservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright
fixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a
deliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it
without explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files
in the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as "one
DDx PR" but the AC list is the union of nine independent
deliverables.

execute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to
make architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine
sub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC
items; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to
mechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the
shape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Split this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior
attempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,
section "What a follow-up attempt needs"):

  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already
     satisfied by v0.9.23)
  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)
  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)
  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit
     user sign-off in its own bead description)
  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)
  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)
  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)
  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)
  9. routing-followups (AC #8)

Recommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the
split, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3
first — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") confirms
this was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive
execute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further
re-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —
the blocker is bead shape, not capability.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer)
Attempt: 20260429T003007-e136e75e (no-op)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
================
- AC #1 is satisfied by prior work on this branch:
  * cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent at v0.9.23 (verified).
  * CHANGELOG.md (commit 7d50a792) documents the consumed upstream
    capabilities and cites each upstream commit + bead. The changelog
    explicitly notes that upstream shipped routing capabilities incrementally
    across v0.9.10..v0.9.24 rather than as a single tag.
  * All six upstream beads labeled `routing-plan` in
    ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl are CLOSED:
      agent-0dafc7f0 (cost-aware tiebreak)
      agent-191a74f9 (first-class default + local + standard profiles)
      agent-1f46cf22 (routing reference docs)
      agent-53f38d95 (public RouteDecision.Candidates trace)
      agent-bab52778 (supported-models allow-list)
      agent-dfabb10b (typed ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / ErrProfilePinConflict)
  * The needed public types are present in the pinned module at
    /home/erik/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/agent@v0.9.23/routing_errors.go:
      ErrHarnessModelIncompatible, ErrProfilePinConflict, ErrNoProfileCandidate,
      ErrUnknownProfile, ErrNoLiveProvider, DecisionWithCandidates interface
      with RouteCandidates() []RouteCandidate.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING THIS ATTEMPT
=================================
The bead is an epic-tracker, not a single deliverable. AC #2 through #10
collectively require — in one PR with no compensating paths retained — all of:

  D1. workerDispatchAdapter.DispatchWorker rewrite + Drain-button parity.
  D2. Removal of default-path tier-ladder synthesis from
      ddx agent run / ddx agent execute-bead / ddx work; ResolveProfileLadder
      and ResolveTierModelRef gated behind --escalate only.
  D3. Preflight gate inserted before beadStore.Claim in execute_bead_loop.go
      (the bead points at line 352, which has already drifted — the actual
      Claim site is now line 341; the loop is 1052 lines and has substantial
      surrounding dispatch state to thread through).
  D4. Hard-error breaking change for `agent.routing.default_harness`;
      one-time warnings + default-path ignore for `profile_ladders` and
      `model_overrides`. Schema deletion in
      cli/internal/config/schema/config.schema.json. Config-load test fixtures.
  D5. CLI surface parity for ddx agent run (no flags / --profile / --harness /
      --model / combos), ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead.

  Plus tests demanded by the AC:
  - AC #2 integration test reproducing the original 19-burn drain failure
    against the user's real config shape.
  - AC #3 worker-level failure record assertion with seeded pathological route
    via an upstream test seam that does not currently exist on the DDx side.
  - AC #5 four end-to-end override-behavior tests (incl. cost-tiebreak
    seeded-providers test).
  - AC #6 Playwright golden for the drain-queue UI path
    (cli/internal/server/frontend); fixture must mirror user's real
    endpoint config.
  - AC #7 dead-code detector wired into CI catching orphaned ladder helpers.
  - AC #10 regression-guard integration test that fails before this work and
    succeeds after.

  Plus the directive in AC #7 — "Search DDx for code that re-implements any
  of: allow-list checks, exact-pin filtering, profile-to-preference mapping,
  provider cost scoring. Must be absent. If found, PR fails review." —
  requires a deliberate audit + removal pass spanning at least:
      cli/internal/agent/harness_config.go

This is a multi-thousand-line, multi-subsystem refactor with a hard
breaking-change config migration and a new Playwright golden. It cannot be
landed responsibly in a single execute-bead pass without a much narrower
sub-bead breakdown. Attempting it as one commit risks: half-finished tier-
ladder removal that leaves the default path in an undefined state; a config
schema break that hard-errors existing user configs without the migration
warnings actually firing; or merging a Playwright golden whose fixture
silently passes because the underlying dispatch path was not fully rewired.

The previous attempt already correctly scoped this bead to AC #1 only and
documented the upstream prerequisite. Doing more here without
decomposition would either produce a bad commit or violate
DOWITYTD on a bead whose own description acknowledges it is a
"DDx-side tracker only" for an upstream-driven epic.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT NEEDS
==================================
Decompose ddx-fdd3ea36 into independently shippable sub-beads, each
small enough to land in one execute-bead pass with focused tests:

  ddx-<new>-D1: workerDispatchAdapter + Drain button — empty-spec defaults to
                Profile:"default"; no other synthesis. Unit test +
                dashboard wiring. Closes AC #2 partially.

  ddx-<new>-D2a: Default path stops calling ResolveTierModelRef /
                 ResolveProfileLadder. Add --escalate flag that re-enables
                 them. Tests assert call graph. Closes AC #2 fully.

  ddx-<new>-D3: Preflight gate before Claim using upstream's typed
                ErrHarnessModelIncompatible. Targeted test using upstream
                test seam (file the upstream test-seam ask as a separate
                ddx-agent bead if missing). Closes AC #3.

  ddx-<new>-D4: Config breaking-change migration. Hard-error on
                default_harness; warn-and-ignore on profile_ladders/
                model_overrides; schema update; migration fixture tests;
                CHANGELOG migration guide. Closes AC #4.

  ddx-<new>-D5: CLI flag-surface parity tests for ddx agent run / work /
                execute-bead with the four override permutations from AC #5.
                Closes AC #5.

  ddx-<new>-Audit: Audit pass for AC #7 (compensating logic removal +
                   dead-code orphan detector in CI). Closes AC #7.

  ddx-<new>-Regression: AC #10 regression-guard integration test that
                        reproduces the 19-burn drain bug; AC #2's
                        integration sibling. Closes AC #10 (and validates
                        D1+D2+D3 together).

  ddx-<new>-Playwright: AC #6 frontend golden against the rewired Drain
                        path. Last in sequence — depends on D1+D2+D3 +
                        Regression. Closes AC #6.

  ddx-<new>-Docs: Cross-link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream profile
                  catalog + override precedence + best-provider contract
                  docs (AC #9 second half — CHANGELOG half is already in
                  CHANGELOG.md from the prior commit).

  ddx-<new>-D6-followup: Decision-trace consumers in route-status /
                         providers UI — explicitly out of scope per the
                         bead description but should be filed now so the
                         tracking exists (bead description's own AC #8
                         requires this).

ddx-fdd3ea36 should remain open as the umbrella tracker until all
sub-beads close, at which point AC #2..#10 can be re-verified end-to-end
in one final review pass.

NO CODE CHANGES IN THIS ATTEMPT.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer epic)

Disposition: no_changes — this is an epic/tracker bead whose work has been
deliberately decomposed into nine child beads. Some are closed; four remain
open. The tracker cannot close until those four close. There is no
implementation work that belongs at the epic level itself.

================================================================================
What is done
================================================================================

Upstream prerequisite (AC #1):
  All six routing-plan beads in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/ are closed and merged
  into tagged ddx-agent releases (v0.9.10..v0.9.24):
    agent-191a74f9  profiles (default/local/standard)         closed
    agent-bab52778  supported-models allow-list + gating      closed
    agent-dfabb10b  ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / PinConflict closed
    agent-53f38d95  public RouteDecision.Candidates           closed
    agent-0dafc7f0  CostUSDPer1kTokens + cost tiebreak        closed
    agent-1f46cf22  routing documentation                     closed
  cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23, which contains
  every routing-plan commit (verified via `git tag --contains <sha>` against
  closing_commit_sha for each upstream bead).

DDx-side child beads already closed:
  ddx-967e9802  routing-bump (go.mod pin + CHANGELOG)         closed
  ddx-755f5881  routing-default-path (D1 + D2)                closed   → AC #2
  ddx-98e6e9ef  routing-preflight-gate (D3)                   closed   → AC #3
  ddx-dd4c423d  routing-drain-regression test                 closed   → AC #10

Code evidence the closed work is in tree:
  cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go:20-21
    test seams verify ResolveProfileLadder/ResolveTierModelRef are NOT called
    on the default path (per ddx-755f5881 AC #3)
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:84-120
    DispatchWorker normalizes empty input to {Profile: "default"} only
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:360-392
    routing preflight gate runs BEFORE Claim at line 394 (D3, ddx-98e6e9ef)
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1683-1684
    --escalate / --override-model flags exist; tier ladder + model_overrides
    are off by default (consistent with D2/D4 design)
  cli/internal/config/types.go:230-234
    migration note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in ddx-87fb72c2

================================================================================
What is open (blocks tracker close)
================================================================================

ddx-87fb72c2  routing-config-deprecation                      open  → AC #4
              hard error on default_harness; ladders/overrides opt-in only;
              CHANGELOG migration entry. (Note: the field-removal half appears
              landed per types.go:230-234 — verify what residual scope this
              bead still owns before executing it.)

ddx-2589e256  routing-overrides-e2e                           open  → AC #5
              --harness/--model/--profile flag plumbing parity tests for
              ResolveRoute(Profile, Harness, Model) shape; cost-tiebreak e2e;
              typed-error surfacing for incompatible combos.

ddx-950321ef  routing-frontend-goldens                        open  → AC #6
              Playwright drain-queue fixtures matching the real failing
              config; Drain button → live-endpoint-backed harness;
              never gemini+non-gemini-model; never 19-burn.

ddx-653f6ac9  routing-cleanup                                 open  → AC #7
              dead-code detector for orphaned compensating logic;
              remove residual allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring code
              that re-implements upstream behavior.

ddx-a0cf398b  routing-followups                               open  → AC #8
              file the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer
              bead; close out D6/D7 housekeeping.

================================================================================
Why this bead does not get implementation in this pass
================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item
   (#4, #5, #6, #7, #8, plus the documentation half of #9) is the contract of
   a specific open child bead. Implementing them here would either duplicate
   work that the child bead will redo, or steal scope that another worker
   may already be planning to claim.

2. The bead description's "Sequencing" section explicitly orders:
     1. upstream lands (done)
     2. go.mod bump (done — ddx-967e9802)
     3. D1+D2+D3+D5 land together (D1+D2=ddx-755f5881 done; D3=ddx-98e6e9ef
        done; D5=ddx-2589e256 OPEN)
     4. D4 (ddx-87fb72c2) lands with or right after the above (OPEN)
     5. follow-ups (ddx-a0cf398b, ddx-950321ef, ddx-653f6ac9) (OPEN)
   The tracker is meant to close once steps 3–5 complete via their child
   beads, not to carry independent code.

3. Attempting any of {D4, override e2e, playwright goldens, dead-code sweep}
   in this single execute-bead pass would land a sprawling cross-cutting
   commit that violates the per-child-bead acceptance contracts and is
   hard to review. Per CLAUDE.md "DOWITYTD" and the merge-policy memory,
   the right action is to execute the children individually.

================================================================================
What a follow-up attempt should do
================================================================================

This tracker should NOT be re-queued for direct execution. Instead:

  a) Execute the four open child beads in their natural order:
        ddx-87fb72c2 (D4 — config deprecation residue + CHANGELOG)
        ddx-2589e256 (D5 — override flag e2e)
        ddx-950321ef (D6-equivalent — Playwright goldens)
        ddx-653f6ac9 (cleanup / dead-code sweep)
        ddx-a0cf398b (file follow-up beads)
     Each closes one or two AC items on this tracker.

  b) When all five close, re-open this tracker briefly to:
        - verify every AC item maps to a closed child or in-tree evidence
        - update DDx CHANGELOG (AC #9) if not already done by ddx-967e9802
        - close ddx-fdd3ea36 with a closing-comment that points at each
          child bead's closing commit.

That close step is documentation/bookkeeping — a single small commit, well
suited to a future execute-bead pass once the children are green.

No code changes were made in this pass. Workspace is clean.

rationale: status: already_satisfied
verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/federation/ -run TestFanOut -count=1 -timeout 60s

This bead (ddx-fe63969f) duplicates work already committed under bead
ddx-89bc2db4 in commit ef7efd46 ("feat(federation): hub-side fan-out
client/merger with timeout, max-concurrency, partial results").

Acceptance-criteria mapping to existing code:

AC1 — Fan-out client with timeout + concurrency bounds.
  FanOutClient at cli/internal/federation/fanout.go:125-141 with
  MaxConcurrency (default 8) + PerNodeTimeout (default 5s). Concurrency
  is enforced by a buffered channel semaphore at fanout.go:226. Per-node
  timeout is applied via context.WithTimeout in callOne at fanout.go:329.

AC2 — Merger marks partials; never silently drops.
  FanOutResult at fanout.go:103-122 splits results into Responses,
  Errors, Skipped, StatusUpdates, and Nodes. Every input spoke produces
  exactly one NodeResult (fanout.go:225, 266-289). NodeOutcomeKind
  values OutcomeTimeout / OutcomeOffline / OutcomeHTTPError /
  OutcomeSkipped (fanout.go:46-65) distinguish partial-failure modes.
  Per-node timeouts deliberately do NOT flip status to offline
  (fanout.go:353-361), preserving the registry's stale signal.

AC3 — Per-spoke capability check skips incompatible spokes gracefully.
  preflightSkip at fanout.go:295-323 filters by RequiredCapabilities
  against SpokeRecord.Capabilities (ignoring the synthetic @identity:
  fingerprint cap), emitting OutcomeSkipped + SkipReasonMissingCapability.
  Version incompatibility is also handled (SkipReasonIncompatibleVersion
  via the Handshake gate).

AC4 — Tests cover timeout, partial, and capability skip.
  cli/internal/federation/fanout_test.go covers:
    - TestFanOut_PerNodeTimeoutDoesNotBlockOthers (timeout)
    - TestFanOut_PartialResultOnOneFailure (partial)
    - TestFanOut_StaleVsOfflineDistinct (partial / status-update policy)
    - TestFanOut_MidResponseDisconnect (partial / transport failure)
    - TestFanOut_HTTPErrorIsNotOffline (partial / classification)
    - TestFanOut_MissingCapabilitySkipped (capability skip)
    - TestFanOut_VersionIncompatibleSpokesSkipped (version skip)
    - TestFanOut_MaxConcurrencyEnforced (concurrency bound)
    - TestFanOut_AllSpokesSucceed, TestFanOut_PostsGraphQLPayload,
      TestFanOut_RejectsEmptyRequest (happy path / contract).

The verification command above re-runs the suite and exits 0 when all
AC-covering tests pass.

docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md:54:6. **Context rot is real; bounded context execution is the response.** LLM output quality decays as a single context window fills — well before the hard token limit. Long-running agent sessions accumulate transcript, tool noise, and failed attempts that compete with the original instructions; the agent at hour one is not the agent at minute one. The structural fix is **bounded context execution** — also known as the **Ralph loop**: every unit of work runs in a fresh, narrowly-scoped context against an explicit contract (a bead with acceptance criteria), with persistent state landing on disk as evidence rather than carried forward as transcript. DDx implements this with `ddx agent execute-bead` (single bounded attempt in an isolated worktree) and `ddx work` (queue drain that re-enters the loop with a fresh context per bead). See `website/content/docs/concepts/bounded-context-execution.md` for the full treatment.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-e658e2ae/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"ddx","body":"All four AC items for ddx-e658e2ae are already satisfied by commit b9b7932d
("fix(beadform): use design tokens; replace inline CSS-var status with badge class [ddx-e658e2ae]")
on the current base. Verified in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/BeadForm.svelte:

  1. inputClass (line 130) uses `text-body-sm` — not `text-sm`.
  2. labelClass (line 131-132) uses `font-label-caps text-label-caps` — not `text-xs`.
  3. Status <select> (lines 174-178) uses `class="{inputClass} {statusBadgeClass(status)}"`,
     where statusBadgeClass returns Tailwind utility classes (`badge-status-open`,
     `badge-status-in-progress`, `badge-status-blocked`, `badge-status-closed`,
     `badge-status-neutral`). No inline `style="background-color: var(--status-…)"`
     remains.
  4. Cancel button (line 269) and Submit button (line 276) both use `text-body-sm`
     — not `text-sm`.

The previous review's BLOCK ("Diff only contains a result.json execution artifact;
no changes to BeadForm.svelte are visible") appears to have inspected only the
execute-bead bundle delta rather than the merged base state. The change landed
in b9b7932d and has not regressed.

No further code changes are warranted. Recommend closing.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ea3a7e23/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead: ddx-ea3a7e23 — V6 generate principle composite (pressure-test gate)

Decision: no_changes. The plan (docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md
§"Bead breakdown" V6) explicitly forbids a manual prompt-file → image fallback:
"No manual prompt-file → image fallback; if those slip, V6 slips." A well-justified
no_changes is preferable to authoring a prompt and shelling out to an image tool
outside the FEAT-010 substrate.

What is done
- Verified the named blocker beads are closed: ddx-d5e71fb3 (FEAT-010 three-layer
  substrate), ddx-5d92b873 (FEAT-007 sidecar + generated_by), ddx-64fbb1b3
  (`ddx artifact regenerate` CLI).
- Verified `ddx artifact regenerate` exists and dispatches into the FEAT-010
  store via cli/cmd/artifact.go: it requires (a) the artifact present in the
  document graph and (b) at least one execution definition that lists the
  artifact under produces_artifact.
- Read the six existing principle prompts under docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/
  (principle-1-lever … principle-6-fulcrum) — V5 author-prompts work is in
  place and can be referenced by the composite prompt.

What is blocking
1. Missing image-generation skill. The `nano-banana-pro-openrouter` skill is
   referenced from `.crush/skills/` and `.pi/skills/` as symlinks pointing to
   `.agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/`, but that target directory does
   not exist in this worktree (only helix-* skills and `ddx` are under
   `.agents/skills/`). Without the skill the generator cannot run.
2. No execution definition registered. `ddx exec list --json` returns `[]`;
   there is no nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator definition, and no
   definition lists `principles-composite` (or any visual artifact id) under
   produces_artifact. `ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite` therefore
   fails with "artifact \"principles-composite\" not found in document graph"
   — the artifact is not registered, and even if it were, selectArtifactGenerator
   in cli/cmd/artifact.go:99 would error with "no generator definition or
   generated_by provenance".
3. Sidecar metadata not authored. The plan calls for
   `principles-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}` co-located with the V5
   prompts, with `depends_on` and `generated_by` edges. The `.ddx.yaml` sidecar
   for the (yet-unrendered) composite cannot be written without a real
   `generated_by` run record, which only the substrate run produces.

What a follow-up attempt would need (in order)
1. A predecessor bead that installs / vendors the `nano-banana-pro-openrouter`
   skill into `.agents/skills/` so the broken symlinks resolve. Today this
   skill is missing from the execute-bead worktree.
2. A predecessor bead that authors a `ddx exec define` execution definition
   for the nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator with
   `produces_artifact: principles-composite` (and, ideally, the six standalone
   principle artifact IDs as well so V7 can reuse it).
3. A predecessor bead that registers the `principles-composite` artifact in
   the document graph (sidecar with id, depends_on .stitch/DESIGN.md and the
   six principle prompts, media_type image/png) so `ddx artifact regenerate`
   resolves it.

Once those three preconditions hold, V6 collapses to: author
docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/principles-composite.prompt.md (referencing the
six existing principle prompts and DESIGN.md tokens), then run
`ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite`. The composite prompt should
explicitly assemble the lever metaphor — beam (P1), cyclic motion (P2),
interchangeable handles (P3), non-uniform load (P4), receipts trail (P5),
fulcrum at the human-AI seam (P6) — so the pressure-test reads on the first
render. Budget tracking ($10 cap, $1.50/image halt) starts when the substrate
run actually executes.

Recommendation: file three predecessor beads above (skill-install, exec-define,
artifact-register), block ddx-ea3a7e23 on them, and re-queue. Do not escalate
to a stronger model — model capability is not the constraint here.

(a) What is done
----------------
AC #1 — 3-way auto-resolution of preserved iteration onto current tip (ort
strategy + -X ours heuristic) before discarding:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:845 landConflictAutoRecover(...)
  Uses `git merge --no-ff -s ort -X ours` in a temp worktree at currentTip,
  aborts cleanly on failure, advances refs/heads/<target> via update-ref +
  SyncWorkTreeToHead on success.

AC #2 — On 3-way failure, dispatch a focused conflict-resolve agent:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:279 ExecuteBeadWorker.ConflictResolver
  func(ctx, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
  Invoked at execute_bead_loop.go:1450 inside runConflictRecovery; on success
  the merge is committed and the bead lands.

AC #3 — Park outcome distinguishes unresolvable vs needs-human:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:264 ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictUnresolvable
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go:270 ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflictNeedsHuman
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1467 sets NeedsHuman when
    ConflictResolver returns isBlocking=true; else Unresolvable.
  Events emitted: kind:land-conflict-unresolvable / kind:land-conflict-needs-human
  (execute_bead_loop.go:1490-1501).

AC #4 — Cooldown 5-30 min, capped under the 24h push-race cap:
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:64
    const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
  Applied at execute_bead_loop.go:824 (parkUntil = now + LandConflictCooldown);
  capLoopCooldown still bounds the value under 24h.

AC #5 — Same code path runs for execution_failed-with-preserved-commit (timeout):
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1397 shouldAttemptConflictRecovery
  Returns true for ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict OR
  ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed when BaseRev != ResultRev and a PreserveRef
  exists. Verified by TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_WithPreservedCommit_AttemptsRecovery.

AC #6 — Regression tests (all pass under `go test ./internal/agent/`):
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_conflict_recover_test.go
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_AutoRecoverySucceeds_BeadCloses (mechanical)
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_AutoRecoverFails_EscalatesResolver (structural)
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_BlockingResolver_NeedsHuman
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_WithPreservedCommit_AttemptsRecovery
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_NoPreserveRef_SkipsRecovery
    - TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_EmptyProjectRoot_SkipsRecovery

AC #7 — CHANGELOG entry: present (search "execute-loop: conflict-recovery for
preserved iterations before discard" in CHANGELOG.md).

AC #8 — FEAT-006 updated:
  docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md:76-96 lists the new
  structured outcomes land_conflict_unresolvable / land_conflict_needs_human
  and the 3-way → focused-resolve → park escalation ladder.

Verification command run inside this worktree:
  go test ./internal/agent/ -run \
    "TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict|TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_With|TestExecuteBeadLoopExecutionFailed_NoPreserveRef|TestExecuteBeadLoopLandConflict_EmptyProjectRoot" \
    -count=1
  → ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent  0.031s
The full agent test suite also passes; the only failure is
TestIntegration_OpencodeEcho (unrelated, pre-existing 60s harness timeout).

(b) What is blocking
--------------------
Nothing implementation-wise is blocking. The bead's <notes> review block
("Diff at rev 1550c5382a is a bare merge commit with no code changes")
appears to refer to a prior attempt whose merge commit carried no code
because the actual implementation had already landed in an earlier attempt
on main. The current base revision (ee729c4a) already contains all the
code, tests, and docs required by the AC. Producing a synthetic edit just
to satisfy the "must commit" expectation would either duplicate or
deteriorate the existing implementation.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need
---------------------------------------
A follow-up attempt should:
  1. Confirm via `git log --oneline -- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go
     cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
     cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_conflict_recover_test.go` that the
     implementation commits (predating ee729c4a) are reachable from main.
  2. If they are reachable, close ddx-0097af14 directly with a
     closing_commit_sha pointing at the most recent of those implementation
     commits — no new attempt is needed.
  3. If reviewers want stronger evidence than the existing tests provide
     (e.g. an end-to-end test against a real git repo demonstrating ort
     auto-resolution of a real positional drift conflict), open a new bead
     scoped to that test rather than re-running this one.

.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-005-metric-runtime-history.md:188:## Ratchet Evaluation and execute-bead Landing
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-005-metric-runtime-history.md:190:Metric ratchets feed into `ddx agent execute-bead` merge/preserve decisions.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-005-metric-runtime-history.md:213:### Acceptance Criteria (ratchet evaluation feeding execute-bead)
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-005-metric-runtime-history.md:215:- Given a metric execution run completes with a value exceeding `thresholds.ratchet` in the blocking direction, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then the iteration is classified as not merge-eligible and is preserved under a hidden ref rather than landed.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-005-metric-runtime-history.md:216:- Given a metric execution run completes with a value between `thresholds.warn` and `thresholds.ratchet`, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then a warning is emitted but the iteration remains merge-eligible.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-005-metric-runtime-history.md:217:- Given a non-required, non-ratcheted metric execution run completes, when execute-bead evaluates the outcome, then the run is stored as evidence and does not affect the merge/preserve decision.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ebf3c09b/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead ddx-ebf3c09b — repo-wide verb migration + agent_*.go cleanup
===================================================================

NO CHANGES committed in this attempt.

## Why this bead is currently blocked

The bead description has four parts. Three of the four are blocked on a
prerequisite that has not landed:

(a) "Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands"
    (agent_list/capabilities/doctor/check/models/providers/route_status/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay)

(c) "Register DDx overrides on mounted subtree (deprecations + condense)"

Both presuppose a mounted upstream Cobra root grafted under `ddx agent`
— i.e. `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))` per FEAT-001-cli.md:69 and
plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md:28.

The CONTRACT-003 audit (committed 08a40b69) explicitly identifies this
as a gap (docs/helix/02-design/contract-003-audit-2026-04-29.md:23–89):

    > DDx cannot write `agentcli.NewRootCommand()` against the current
    > contract.
    >
    > The plan's "delete with prejudice" list (agent_list,
    > agent_capabilities, agent_doctor, agent_check, agent_models,
    > agent_providers, agent_route_status, agent_catalog,
    > agent_reindex) presupposes that upstream owns these subcommands
    > inside a mountable tree. Today they exist only as flag-driven
    > branches in cmd/agent/main.go that DDx cannot consume
    > programmatically.
    >
    > Plan bead #6 ("open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent`
    > if #5 surfaces gaps") is **required, not conditional**, because
    > of this gap.

Concretely:

  * cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 123–141) wires every soon-to-be-deleted
    file via `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgentCheckCommand())`,
    `newAgentModelsCommand()`, `newAgentProvidersCommand()`,
    `newAgentRouteStatusCommand()`, `newAgentListCommand()`,
    `newAgentCapabilitiesCommand()`, `newAgentDoctorCommand()`,
    `newAgentBenchmarkCommand()`, `newAgentCatalogCommand()`,
    `newAgentReplayCommand()`. Deleting any of those `agent_*.go`
    files without the upstream `MountCLI` replacement would leave
    agent_cmd.go referencing missing constructors and break the build.
  * The replacement (`cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`) requires
    the upstream module to expose `MountCLI` from a non-`internal/`
    package, which the audit specifies as the upstream amendment
    CONTRACT-003-A (port `cmd/agent/main.go` from `flag` to `cobra`,
    move into `agent/cli`, re-export `MountCLI`, bump module version).
    That work has not happened in `~/Projects/agent`.

(b) "Migrate DDx-only subcommands"
    (agent_executions → tries_*; agent_log → runs_log;
     agent_metrics → runs_metrics; agent_usage folded into runs_metrics;
     agent_workers → work_workers)

This portion is mechanically possible inside DDx alone, but per the
plan (line 208) beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically — with #15
landing in isolation, the new namespaces (`ddx runs`, `ddx tries`,
`ddx work workers`) need top-level registration that has no analogue
yet in command_factory_commands.go. The implementation P2 phase of the
plan (lines 196–204) lists "CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work
workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)" as separate work
that comes after the frame/design beads. Doing only (b) here, without
(a) and (c), produces a confusing CLI surface where the same evidence
is reachable both as `ddx agent executions` and `ddx tries list`, and
it strands the deprecation messages that need to point at the new
verbs.

(d) "Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    new verbs"

This depends on the new verbs (`ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx runs *`,
`ddx tries *`, `ddx work workers *`) actually existing as runnable
commands so that the updated scripts/skills run green. `ddx run` and
`ddx try` are not registered today (grep for `Use:.*\"run\"` /
`Use:.*\"try\"` against cli/cmd/*.go shows only the `ddx agent run`
inner command). Updating scripts/eval-skill.sh,
scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh, scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh,
scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call
non-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would
specifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced
this very plan (plan line 208).

## What an unblocked attempt needs

Roughly in dependency order:

1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:
   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,
   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in
   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive — see memory
   feedback_no_local_replace).
2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class
   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the
   plan).
3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons
   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then
   ported in this bead.

Once those are in, the present bead becomes:

  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`
    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,
    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,
    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for
    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).
  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,
    agent_route_status.go (the only "delete with prejudice" files
    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions
    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go
    rewrite).
  * Move agent_executions.go → tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go →
    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);
    agent_workers.go → work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`
    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Update tests in lockstep.
  * Sweep scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    swap old verbs for new (e.g. `ddx agent run` → `ddx run`,
    `ddx agent execute-loop` → `ddx work`,
    `ddx agent execute-bead` → `ddx try`,
    `ddx agent log/metrics/usage/executions/workers` → the new
    `runs/tries/work workers` paths). Specific known references:
    scripts/eval-skill.sh, scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh,
    scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh, scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
    library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md,
    library/personas/README.md, cli/cmd/init.go:757,
    cli/cmd/bead_review.go:30–33, plus a wider grep across
    docs/**/*.md and library/**/*.md.

## What is done in this attempt

Nothing on disk — but the verification above (audit cross-reference,
agent_cmd.go wiring inspection, FEAT-001 surface inspection,
search for top-level `run`/`try` registrations) is captured here so
the next attempt can move directly into execution once bead #6 lands.

## Recommended next steps

- Park this bead behind bead #6 (upstream CONTRACT-003-A amendment)
  and the P2 implementation beads that introduce top-level
  `ddx run` / `ddx try` and the `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`
  namespace skeletons.
- Re-attempt with the same id once those prerequisites have merged.
  At that point this bead is mechanical: a contained refactor of
  agent_cmd.go plus a directed grep-and-edit sweep of the call sites
  enumerated above.
rationale: Bead ddx-ebf3c09b — repo-wide verb migration + agent_*.go cleanup

scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md, etc. to call
non-existent verbs would break those flows immediately and would
specifically break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced
this very plan (plan line 208).

## What an unblocked attempt needs

Roughly in dependency order:

1. Upstream amendment CONTRACT-003-A merged in `~/Projects/agent`:
   port to cobra, move commands to `agent/cli`, export `MountCLI`,
   tag a release, and bump `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` in
   DDx's go.mod (no replace directive — see memory
   feedback_no_local_replace).
2. Top-level `ddx run` and `ddx try` registered as first-class
   commands under CommandFactory (the implementation P2 thread of the
   plan).
3. `ddx runs`, `ddx tries`, `ddx work workers` namespace skeletons
   registered, even if their subcommand implementations are then
   ported in this bead.

Once those are in, the present bead becomes:

  * Replace the 11+ enumerated `cmd.AddCommand(f.newAgent*Command())`
    calls in agent_cmd.go with `cmd.AddCommand(agent.MountCLI(...))`,
    plus deprecation override registrations for `agent run`,
    `agent execute-bead`, `agent execute-loop`, and the override for
    `agent condense` (DDx-specific filter).
  * `git rm` agent_check.go, agent_models.go, agent_providers.go,
    agent_route_status.go (the only "delete with prejudice" files
    that actually exist in DDx today; agent_list/capabilities/doctor/
    catalog/reindex/benchmark/replay only exist as inline functions
    inside agent_cmd.go and would be removed by the agent_cmd.go
    rewrite).
  * Move agent_executions.go → tries_*.go; agent_metrics.go →
    runs_metrics.go (folding agent_usage.go into it);
    agent_workers.go → work_workers.go; rename `newAgentLogCommand`
    in agent_cmd.go into `runs_log.go`. Update tests in lockstep.
  * Sweep scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to
    swap old verbs for new (e.g. `ddx agent run` → `ddx run`,
    `ddx agent execute-loop` → `ddx work`,
    `ddx agent execute-bead` → `ddx try`,
    `ddx agent log/metrics/usage/executions/workers` → the new
    `runs/tries/work workers` paths). Specific known references:
    scripts/eval-skill.sh, scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh,
    scripts/test-doc-snippets.sh, scripts/demos/06-full-journey.sh,
    library/.agents/skills/ddx-refine-plan/SKILL.md,
    library/personas/README.md, cli/cmd/init.go:757,
    cli/cmd/bead_review.go:30–33, plus a wider grep across
    docs/**/*.md and library/**/*.md.

## What is done in this attempt

Nothing on disk — but the verification above (audit cross-reference,
agent_cmd.go wiring inspection, FEAT-001 surface inspection,
search for top-level `run`/`try` registrations) is captured here so
the next attempt can move directly into execution once bead #6 lands.

## Recommended next steps

- Park this bead behind bead #6 (upstream CONTRACT-003-A amendment)
  and the P2 implementation beads that introduce top-level
  `ddx run` / `ddx try` and the `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`
  namespace skeletons.
- Re-attempt with the same id once those prerequisites have merged.
  At that point this bead is mechanical: a contained refactor of
  agent_cmd.go plus a directed grep-and-edit sweep of the call sites
  enumerated above.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-ee2cd434/events.jsonl:12:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes_rationale — bead ddx-ee2cd434 (SD-024 Stage 2)

(a) WHAT IS DONE
----------------
The previous attempt (commit 5ae02964) migrated the high-value `r.Run(RunOptions{...})`
and `RunViaService(..., RunOptions{...})` test sites onto the SD-024 successor API
(`r.RunWithConfig` + `RunWithConfigViaService` + `AgentRunRuntime`) across:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go    (~22 sites: TestRunWith*, TestSessionLogging,
                                         TestRunPromptFile, TestRunStdinMode,
                                         TestRunModel*, TestRunNonZeroExit,
                                         TestRunUnknownHarness, TestRunEmptyPrompt,
                                         TestIntegration_CodexEcho, TestRunOpencode*,
                                         and the *Permissions* pair)
  - cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go   (lmstudio / openrouter dispatch regressions)
  - cli/internal/agent/script_test.go   (runScript helper now builds rcfg via
                                         TestRunConfigOpts and dispatches via
                                         RunWithConfigViaService)

Those 26 sites are the bulk of the "test sites that exercise the high-level agent
run path" called out in the bead description.

(b) WHAT IS BLOCKING
--------------------
The bead AC reads:

    rg 'RunOptions\{|CompareOptions\{|QuorumOptions\{' cli/ returns 0 matches outside
    the type definitions about to be retired.

This is a *global* gate. It cannot be satisfied by test-only changes — it requires
retiring every remaining `RunOptions{...}` construction in both production and test
code. The remaining sites fall into two classes:

CLASS A — tests that call functions whose signature *takes* RunOptions, so the
RunOptions construction is not a "test site" the test could rewrite without
changing the function under test:

  - cli/internal/agent/agent_test.go        BuildArgs(h, RunOptions{...}, model)  — 14 sites
                                            BuildArgs is the function under test.
  - cli/internal/agent/prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go
        line 78  resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile})              — exercises r.resolvePrompt
        line 85  defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})    — exercises pkg-level helper
        line 112 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})  — exercises stub-injection helper
  - cli/internal/agent/session_index_test.go
        line 195 SessionIndexEntryFromResult(root, RunOptions{...}, ...)
        line 278 RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/models_test.go
        line 45  BenchmarkArmsToCompare(arms, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/virtual_test.go
        lines 164/174/214 runVirtualFn(runner, RunOptions{...})
  - cli/internal/agent/claude_stream_test.go
        lines 206/212/218/224/291/324  internal opts: RunOptions{...} fixtures

Migrating any of these test sites requires first changing the *signature* of the
production function under test (BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare,
RunViaServiceWith, runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, SessionIndexEntryFromResult,
plus the internal claude_stream helpers). That is production-code refactor work,
explicitly outside the scope the bead description names.

CLASS B — production callers still constructing RunOptions{...}:

  - cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go              :1462
  - cli/internal/server/server.go     :1913, :3786
  - cli/internal/exec/store.go        :201
  - cli/internal/agent/runner.go      :248, :281, :300, :321, :976
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go :82  (RunWithConfigViaService bridge — by design)
  - cli/internal/agent/compare_adapter.go :61, :261, :414, :685
  - cli/internal/agent/grade.go       :169
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go :706
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go :531

Until these are migrated (and in many cases until the function signatures they
flow through are migrated), the AC's grep gate cannot return zero matches.

The fresh-eyes review on the prior attempt itself concluded:

    Path forward: simplest fix is splitting follow-up beads for
    (a) prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go,
    (b) remaining *_test.go RunOptions sites,
    (c) production-code migration,
    then closing this bead with a narrower AC.

That assessment is correct. The bead title ("5-10 sites") and description ("migrate
test sites … in agent_test.go and any sibling test using RunOptions") describe a
test-scoped refactor; the AC describes a global retirement. The two are not
compatible inside one bead.

(c) WHAT A FOLLOW-UP ATTEMPT WOULD NEED
---------------------------------------
Either:

  Option 1 (preferred): re-cut this bead with a narrowed AC, e.g.

      "rg 'RunOptions\{' cli/internal/agent/{agent,runner,script}_test.go returns
      0 matches in tests that exercise r.Run / RunViaService."

      That AC matches the test-only scope, and the prior commit already satisfies it.

  Option 2: expand bead scope to cover the production migration as well. That is
      a multi-file Stage-2 refactor touching cmd/, internal/server/, internal/exec/,
      and most of internal/agent/. It also requires migrating the signatures of
      BuildArgs, resolvePrompt, defaultResolvePromptForCompare, RunViaServiceWith,
      runVirtualFn, BenchmarkArmsToCompare, and SessionIndexEntryFromResult so the
      test sites in Class A can shed RunOptions. This belongs in its own bead (or
      epic), not in a "5-10 sites" test-migration bead.

No new code is committed in this attempt because:

  1. The work the bead description actually scopes is already done (5ae02964).
  2. Doing test-only edits that don't move the AC gate would be exploratory churn.
  3. Doing the full production migration here would silently expand the bead scope
     past what the description and title authorize.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-f171a715/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead ddx-f171a715 — operatorPromptSubmit mutation + CSRF + idempotency + identity-bound audit

NO CHANGES RATIONALE
====================

The implementation is already present at the base revision (9cc24280) of this
worktree. It was landed by an earlier attempt in commit 07b6c67e
"feat(server): operatorPromptSubmit GraphQL mutation with CSRF + idempotency +
identity audit [ddx-f171a715]" and merged to main in 4d6db126.

The previous review BLOCK ("diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no schema, resolver, CSRF, idempotency, audit, or test code is
present") inspected an attempt diff that did not actually carry the code. The
code in fact merged and is currently present at HEAD.

Evidence each AC is satisfied
-----------------------------

AC1. operatorPromptSubmit mutation in schema.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:2965 declares the mutation.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/models.go:1261,1271 generated input/result types.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go:169
    `(*mutationResolver).OperatorPromptSubmit` is the resolver.

AC2. CSRF required (returns 403 without).
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:162 errCSRF carries "(status: 403)".
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:180-189 rejects when the request has no
    *http.Request in ctx, missing header, or token mismatch.
  - StaticCSRFTokenStore.Validate (line 148) is constant-time.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_CSRF (3 subcases — missing/wrong/valid).

AC3. Idempotency key dedupes within 24h window.
  - OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL = 24h (resolver_operator_prompt.go:23).
  - MemoryIdempotencyCache.Lookup/Store enforce TTL eviction (lines 92-118).
  - Resolver short-circuits on cache hit returning Deduplicated=true (lines
    197-207) and stores key->beadID after successful create (line 237).
  - Tests: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_Idempotency (same key dedupes, different
    keys do not, no second audit event on dedup); TestMemoryIdempotencyCache_
    TTLEviction (locks 24h boundary at 23h present / 25h evicted).

AC4. Audit event includes identity.
  - resolver_operator_prompt.go:218-235 appends a BeadEvent of kind
    "operator_prompt_submit" whose Body records identity kind, RemoteAddr,
    X-Tailscale-User, X-Tailscale-Node, and idempotency_key, and whose Actor
    is set from operatorPromptIdentity (tsnet user OR localhost:<remote>).
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts EventsByKind returns
    exactly one event with non-empty Actor and Body containing identity= and
    idempotency_key=key-1.

AC5. Default = proposed (not ready).
  - bead.NewOperatorPromptBead (internal/bead/types.go:163) sets
    Status = StatusProposed and IssueType = IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.
  - Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath asserts Status == StatusProposed
    and IssueType == IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.

AC6. Tests cover all five.
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt_test.go has four
    tests (HappyPath, CSRF with 3 subtests, Idempotency, TTLEviction) which
    together exercise AC1–AC5.

Verification
------------

  $ go test ./internal/server/graphql/ -run 'OperatorPrompt|MemoryIdempotency' -v -count=1
  === RUN   TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath
  --- PASS: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_HappyPath
  === RUN   TestOperatorPromptSubmit_CSRF
      --- PASS: missing_header_rejected
      --- PASS: wrong_token_rejected
      --- PASS: valid_token_accepted
  === RUN   TestOperatorPromptSubmit_Idempotency
  --- PASS: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_Idempotency
  === RUN   TestMemoryIdempotencyCache_TTLEviction
  --- PASS: TestMemoryIdempotencyCache_TTLEviction
  PASS
  ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server/graphql 0.028s

Conclusion
----------

No code changes are warranted. The bead's contract is fully met by code
already present at HEAD. Re-running the review against the current tree
(rather than a single attempt's diff) should resolve the BLOCK.

Stage E1 implementation is complete and well-structured. The `mcpText` helper consolidates ~100 literal sites into a single bounded construction point, with a self-enforcing AST test (`TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage`) that prevents regression. REST handlers (`handleReadDocument`, `handleAgentWorkerPrompt` — switched to JSON, `handleAgentSessionDetail`) and `loadExecutionBundleDetail` all clamp via `evidence.ClampOutput`. rationale: status: already_satisfied

Every AC item for ddx-f3c7149d (S15-4) is already implemented and merged.
The REVIEW:BLOCK note on this bead is stale: it describes an earlier
attempt whose diff contained only an execution result.json. A later
attempt landed the actual code in commit 7c1ff6a5
("feat(graphql): enforce per-project allowlist on ts-net operator-prompt
submit [ddx-f3c7149d]") and merge commit 804f6dcf, both of which are
already in HEAD's history (base 75e0d9472387 includes them).

AC mapping (all in cli/internal/server/graphql/):

1. Approve mutation transitions proposed -> open (trusted only):
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:398 OperatorPromptApprove
     - requires CSRF, requires canAutoApprove(ident) for the caller
     - rejects non-operator-prompt or non-proposed beads
     - sets bead.StatusOpen and appends OperatorPromptApproveEventKind
   Test: resolver_operator_prompt_lifecycle_test.go
     TestOperatorPromptApprove_TransitionsProposedToOpen

2. Cancel transitions proposed -> cancelled:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:446 OperatorPromptCancel
     - sets bead.StatusCancelled and appends OperatorPromptCancelEventKind
   Test: TestOperatorPromptCancel_TransitionsProposedToCancelled

3. auto-approve config skips proposed for configured localhost identity
   only and never for ts-net by default:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:533 canAutoApprove
     - returns false unconditionally when ident.kind != "localhost"
     - empty allowlist -> false; "localhost" sentinel matches any actor
     OperatorPromptSubmit branch at resolver_operator_prompt.go:361 only
     calls canAutoApprove when input.AutoApprove=true.
   Tests:
     TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet
     TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost
     TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedWhenIdentityNotOnAllowlist

4. Per-project allowlist enforced; non-listed ts-net peers get 403 on
   submit:
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:513 canSubmit
     - localhost always allowed; ts-net only when actor in allowlist
     resolver_operator_prompt.go:233 errSubmitDenied (status 403)
   Test: TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetIdentityNotInAllowlistDenied

5. Config wiring:
     server/server.go:116-170 operatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist field +
     parseOperatorPromptAllowlistEnv(DDX_OPERATOR_PROMPT_ALLOWLIST)
     server/server.go:4709 plumbed into Resolver
     SetOperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist setter for tests

.ddx/attachments/ddx-dc2249e9/events.jsonl:9:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-dc2249e9 — no_changes rationale

What is done
============
The acceptance criterion is already satisfied at the base revision
(d01bc4453e9913335945ea1461485b5c0516a5bb).

  cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/executions.spec.ts:362-406

contains the test:

  test('execution detail renders cost tier information', async ({ page }) => { ... })

It loads the execution detail page (/nodes/.../executions/<id>), routes the
NodeInfo / Projects / ExecutionDetail GraphQL operations, asserts the heading
matches the execution id, and asserts the Cost quick-fact cell is present and
renders "$0.0400" — the value fmtCost(0.04) produces from blockExec.costUsd.

The Cost quick-fact cell is rendered unconditionally by the detail page at
cli/.../executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte:188-189 via fmtCost (defined at
:127-130 as `$${c.toFixed(4)}`), so the assertion is grounded in the actual UI.

This test was added in commit 472d4aaf
("test(e2e): verify cost tier info renders in execution detail [ddx-dc2249e9]")
and merged into main via 4bab0a16. It is present on the current base revision.

Why no new commit
=================
The prior REVIEW:BLOCK note ("Diff contains only an execution result.json
artifact; no test file changes are present") referred to the most recent
attempt (20260501T035858), which produced only an evidence artifact because
the test had already landed in 472d4aaf. The bead was re-queued, but the AC is
already met on main — there is no further test code to add or change in this
worktree without duplicating the existing test.

Adding a duplicate or trivially-renamed test would be churn, not progress.
The existing test fully covers the acceptance criterion: it lives in
executions.spec.ts, verifies cost-tier information is rendered in the
execution detail view, and is wired into the same Playwright suite that runs
in CI.

What a follow-up would need
===========================
If the reviewer believes the existing test is insufficient, the follow-up
should specify which additional dimension is missing — e.g. asserting on a
distinct MinPower/MaxPower field that does not yet exist in the UI, or
asserting on the Cost cell from the execution-list row rather than the
detail view. As written, the AC ("MinPower/MaxPower OR cost tier
information is rendered in execution detail view; test passes in CI") is
satisfied by the cost-tier branch of the disjunction.
