- [ ] Watchdog reaps workers past `WatchdogDeadline` with no phase transition in `StallDeadline`
- [ ] SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation works on an intentionally-wedged test worker (unit test with a goroutine that ignores ctx)
- [ ] Reaped worker's bead claim is released (tracker status returns to open or claim-expired)
- [ ] `bead.reaped` event recorded on tracker with reason="watchdog" and duration
- [ ] Watchdog deadlines are configurable via ddx server config","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:48:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"13cef95535aea82c1f00dd596704f141ed131af7","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:43:00.09109294Z","description":"## Residual risk after RC1–RC4 from ddx-0a651925

Even with context propagation (RC1), wall-clock bounds (RC2), tightened breakers (RC3), and provider deadlines (RC4), a goroutine-level deadlock or panic-swallowed handler could still leave a worker alive past any reasonable bound. Today, `server.WorkerManager.Stop` (workers.go:648) only calls `handle.cancel()` — it has no escalation to OS-level signal and no autonomous trigger. ddx-b808df39 adds the *operator* tool; this bead adds the *autonomous* watchdog.

## Scope

1. WorkerManager runs a supervisor goroutine that, every minute, inspects each `workerHandle`.
2. If `time.Since(record.StartedAt) > WatchdogDeadline` (default e.g. 6h) AND `record.CurrentAttempt` has not transitioned in `StallDeadline` (default e.g. 1h), escalate:
   - Send SIGTERM to the worker's process group (requires surfacing PID, aligned with ddx-b808df39).
   - After 30s grace, send SIGKILL.
   - Mark record.State = "reaped"; update claimed bead state so the claim is not leaked.
3. Emit a `bead.reaped` event on the tracker with the full timeline so post-mortems are easy.

## Non-goals

- Per-bead runtime tuning; defaults are enough.
- Replacing ddx-b808df39 — this is defense-in-depth, not duplication. ddx-b808df39 is operator-initiated; this bead is system-initiated.

## Dependencies

- ddx-b808df39 surfaces PID in `ddx agent workers --json` output; this bead reuses that plumbing.","events_attachment":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-34d039b7/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T06:02:55.327228469Z","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-34d039b7","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:enhancement","area:agent","area:workers","root-cause:defense-in-depth"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-0a651925","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-6ca39274","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: autonomous worker watchdog with process-level reaper","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:03:25.675050303Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:2:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Audit report (checked-in .md or bead-comment) lists every `http.Client.Do`-equivalent call in agent providers with its current deadline semantics
- [ ] Each main provider call path installs a per-request deadline (default 15m or catalog-configurable)
- [ ] Streaming body reads terminate within a bounded idle period (ReadDeadline or equivalent)
- [ ] Integration test: fake HTTP server accepts the connection, sends headers, then stops responding. Provider call returns within deadline with a distinct error","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:30:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"b0a4fce4796e6841c9a9686ebeee197fb22a2fa7","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:42:38.821147268Z","description":"## Root cause RC4 from ddx-0a651925

After RC1 is fixed (caller ctx propagates), provider HTTP calls still need per-request deadlines. A stalled TCP socket that delivers headers and then stops sending body bytes — particularly during streaming token reads — can hang a goroutine for days.

Grepping the `DocumentDrivenDX/agent@v0.3.14` module cache shows short timeouts only on discovery probes (`provider/openai/discovery.go:46,174`, `openai.go:123`). The main `Run` call path inside agent providers appears to inherit whatever ctx DDx passes — which today is `context.Background()` (see RC1).

## Scope

1. Audit `openai`, `anthropic`, `virtual` providers in the agent library for per-request deadline usage.
2. Install a per-request deadline (default ~10-15 min) on each provider call; separate from the wall-clock bound in RC2.
3. For streaming bodies, enforce an **idle-read limit** on the response body so a mid-stream stall cannot hold a goroutine indefinitely (e.g. `http.Transport.ResponseHeaderTimeout` + periodic `SetReadDeadline` on the underlying conn).
4. Surface provider-timeout failures as a distinct status in `ExecuteBeadResult.Detail`.

## Non-goals

- Rewriting providers; minimally invasive change only.
- Changing DDx-side timeout defaults (RC2 covers outer bound).

## Prerequisite

Sibling RC1 bead must be resolved first, otherwise adding deadlines inside providers is useless while DDx still feeds them `context.Background()`.","events_attachment":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-3e3913a0/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T05:47:50.145743886Z","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-3e3913a0","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:bug","area:agent","area:providers","root-cause:rc4"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-0a651925","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-b67b87ce","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: audit and enforce per-request HTTP deadlines on agent provider calls","updated_at":"2026-04-18T05:48:25.052955138Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:3:{"acceptance":"- [ ] `RunOptions` accepts a caller ctx, or `Runner.Run`/`Runner.RunAgent` accept ctx explicitly
- [ ] The internal cancel is derived from the caller ctx (no `context.Background()` seeds inside Run/RunAgent)
- [ ] `agent.ExecuteBead` takes ctx and passes it down
- [ ] `server.WorkerManager.Stop(id)` cancels the running agent provider call within 2s in a unit test (fake provider blocks on ctx)
- [ ] Regression test: runner with a canceled ctx returns promptly (<500ms) regardless of mocked provider latency","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:51:41Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"9b044bcebc981533d2d48d4ffd79a91743dee884","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:24.464808955Z","description":"## Root cause RC1 from ddx-0a651925

`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:239` (Runner.Run) and `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:163` (Runner.RunAgent) both call `context.WithCancel(context.Background())`, discarding any context from the caller. `RunOptions` exposes no ctx field. `agent.ExecuteBead` likewise takes no ctx, so `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s ctx never reaches the agent call.

As a result, `WorkerManager.Stop(id)` (server/workers.go:648) cancels only the worker goroutine; the embedded `agentlib.Run` call (and any subprocess it launched) keeps running until SIGKILL. This is the structural defect behind 33–142h hung workers.

## Scope

1. Add `Context context.Context` to `RunOptions` (or change signatures to `Run(ctx, opts)`). Default to `context.Background()` when nil.
2. Derive the internal ctx from the caller's rather than `context.Background()`.
3. Thread ctx through `agent.ExecuteBead` → `runner.Run` → `agentlib.Run` → provider HTTP.
4. Update `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s `singleTierAttempt` closure to pass `ctx` into `agent.ExecuteBead`.

## Non-goals

- New wall-clock timeout logic (covered by sibling RC2 bead)
- Operator-facing stop command (covered by ddx-b808df39)

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:4:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Compaction-stuck detection fires within 15 minutes when no-op compaction events are the only activity, regardless of event rate
- [ ] No regression on the existing behaviour for healthy agents (normal runs do not trip)
- [ ] Unit test: fake agentlib emits one no-op compaction event every 3 seconds → breaker fires within 15 min
- [ ] Result.json `detail` clearly reports the time-based breaker when it fires","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-19T22:40:19Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"721226","closing_commit_sha":"7c69dc3ec37cdc89ddbaf2ce961001d451a32304","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:53.916108666Z","description":"## Root cause RC3 from ddx-0a651925

`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:218-241` trips at `stuckThreshold = maxConsecutiveCompactionFailures` (50 by default). Observed on bundle `20260416T230316-efbf91c5`: the breaker took **2h 8m** to fire (7,720,908 ms). If each iteration takes ~3 min to emit a no-op compaction event, 50 events = ~2.5h. The counter is useful but fires far too late to be a hang-guard.

## Scope

Either:
1. **Time-bounded** — fire after N no-op events **within M minutes** (e.g. 10 within 15m), OR
2. **Threshold lowered** — drop default from 50 to ~10 (only for agent harness where one iteration is already expensive), OR
3. **Wall-clock guard layered on top** — independent breaker fires after e.g. 20 min of consecutive no-op compaction events regardless of count.

Recommend option 1 or 3: time-based thresholds are more robust to provider-speed variance.

## Non-goals

- Changing the compaction algorithm itself.
- Extending breakers outside the compaction path.","events_attachment":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-b0562380/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-19T22:56:20.038156421Z","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-b0562380","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:enhancement","area:agent","root-cause:rc3"],"owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-0a651925","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-e18e2206","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock time","updated_at":"2026-04-19T22:56:41.167757535Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:5:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New default wall-clock deadline (e.g. 3h) applied to both `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` and `Runner.RunAgent`
- [ ] Wall-clock timeout fires even when stdout/stderr/agent events are flowing (unit test: fake provider emits 1 event/sec for > deadline, expect cancel at deadline ± 1s)
- [ ] Idle timeout behaviour preserved (unit test: no activity for idle timeout → fire)
- [ ] Result.json distinguishes `timeout: wall-clock` from `timeout: idle`
- [ ] Documented in types.go (DefaultWallClockMS constant with comment)","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T05:15:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"fff2c86240b98cd687a9dc9879926b17411db3f5","created_at":"2026-04-18T04:41:40.15942901Z","description":"## Root cause RC2 from ddx-0a651925

The `DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000` (2h) in `cli/internal/agent/types.go:363` is advertised as a hard cap but implemented as an *idle timeout*:

- `cli/internal/agent/executor.go:154-178` resets `time.NewTimer(idleTimeout)` on every stdout/stderr byte.
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:264-285` resets on every non-compaction agentlib event.

A provider that emits any heartbeat, retry line, or periodic event defeats the timer indefinitely — enabling the 33–142h hangs observed on agent projects.

Only the claude streaming path (`cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:373-384`) uses a true wall-clock deadline via `time.After(timeout)`. That path is why bundle `20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba` fired at exactly 2h 0m 0.098s — and nothing else does.

## Scope

1. Add an absolute wall-clock deadline in addition to (not replacing) the idle timeout.
2. Default the wall-clock bound to something operators can trust — e.g. 3x `DefaultTimeoutMS` or a new `DefaultWallClockMS`.
3. Apply in: `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` (subprocess path) and `Runner.RunAgent` (embedded agent path).
4. Reflect the wall-clock error in result.json as a distinct `error: "wall-clock deadline exceeded after Xh"` so it is not confused with idle timeout.

## Non-goals

- Lowering or removing the existing idle timeout.
- Per-provider tuning (can be follow-up).","events_attachment":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-b203c889/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T05:29:25.624987414Z","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-0a651925-20260418T043148-1346e8a3-b203c889","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:bug","area:agent","area:workers","root-cause:rc2"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-0a651925","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-0acc00fd","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: add wall-clock deadline alongside idle timeout for agent harness","updated_at":"2026-04-18T05:30:49.793036993Z"}

Some provider/model combos don't implement JSON-mode / response_format=json_object / tool-use-required. If a bead prompt depends on structured output, today routing's blindness means the failure surfaces at dispatch.

Per the parent bead's analysis, this is probably covered incidentally by the tool-calling gating sub-bead — most reasoning-tool-capable models also support structured output. This bead exists to either (a) explicitly add a SupportsStructuredOutput capability, or (b) close as 'covered by tool-calling gating' once that bead lands and we verify the assumption.

## Decision gate (do this first)

Before writing code, verify the 'covered by tool-calling' hypothesis:
- Survey the providers we currently route to (anthropic, openai, openrouter, omlx, lmstudio, ollama) and check whether each one's tool-calling support implies JSON-mode support.
- If yes everywhere we care about: close this bead with a note in the tool-calling bead's tests / docs that asserts the implication.
- If no (any counter-example): proceed with scope below.

## Scope (only if decision gate fails)

- Add SupportsStructuredOutput tri-state to ModelEntry / Surface and to capabilities.go's curated table.
- RouteRequest.RequiresStructuredOutput bool; execute-bead sets it when the prompt requires JSON mode.
- evaluateCandidate rejects RequiresStructuredOutput=true on SupportsStructuredOutput=no. Unknown passes with -v warning.

## Files (only if decision gate fails)

- cli/internal/agent/routing.go
- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go

- Per-provider / per-flavor capability metadata: SupportsTools bool. Two layers:
  1. Hand-curated table for known harnesses (claude, codex, opus, etc.) and provider flavors (openai, anthropic, openrouter, omlx, lmstudio, ollama).

- RouteRequest gains RequiresTools bool (default false to preserve current behavior; execute-bead sets true).

## Out of scope

- Context-window, structured-output, effort, permissions gating (sibling beads).
- Probing servers actively at routing time (uses cached discovery + table).

- cli/internal/agent/types.go (RouteRequest.RequiresTools, capability fields on CandidatePlan)
- cli/internal/agent/routing.go (rejection in rejectIfNotViable / evaluateCandidate)
- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go (SupportsTools on ModelEntry / Surface)

Today, Harness.EffortFlag is checked at routing.go:rejectIfNotViable, so harnesses without effort support are rejected when --effort is set. But provider-level gating is missing: e.g., openrouter doesn't honor --effort on non-reasoning models, and --permissions unrestricted may not apply to every harness+provider combination. Routing should reject or downgrade rather than dispatch and silently no-op the flag.

## Scope

- Add effort/permissions capability fields to the curated capability table (same file added by the tool-calling sub-bead, or co-locate here if that one ships first).
  - SupportsEffort tri-state per (provider, model).
  - SupportsPermission(permission_class) — at minimum 'unrestricted'.
- evaluateCandidate / rejectIfNotViable: when RouteRequest names an --effort or --permissions value not honored by the resolved (provider, model) combo, reject with a clear reason. Unknown = pass with -v warning.
- Document expected behavior for each known provider in capabilities.go comments (openrouter non-reasoning models = no effort, etc.).

## Out of scope

- Context-window, tool-calling, structured-output gating (sibling beads).
- Building a 'downgrade' path (e.g., silently strip --effort and continue). Rejection is sufficient for v1; downgrade is a follow-up.

## Files

- cli/internal/agent/routing.go
- cli/internal/agent/capabilities.go (shared with tool-calling bead — or new file if order differs)

Routing currently does not check whether the resolved model's context window can hold the bead prompt. A 32k-context fuzzy match against an 80k-token bead prompt routes 'successfully' and fails mid-dispatch with a provider 400 or truncated output.

## Scope

- Add optional EstimatedPromptTokens (uint32) to RouteRequest (cli/internal/agent/types.go).
- In evaluateCandidate (cli/internal/agent/routing.go), when EstimatedPromptTokens is set and > 0, reject (or penalize) candidates whose concrete_model context window is below it.
- Source of context window per candidate: v0.3.11's LookupModelLimits first, then catalog ModelEntry.ContextWindow (cli/internal/agent/catalog.go) as fallback. If neither is known, treat as 'unknown' — do NOT reject (avoid false negatives), but log at -v.
- Caller side: pass EstimatedPromptTokens from execute-bead when the bead prompt is assembled (best-effort — can use char/4 heuristic if a tokenizer is unavailable).

## Out of scope

- Tool-calling, structured-output, effort, permissions gating (separate sibling beads).
- Adding a tokenizer dependency. char/4 estimate is fine for v1.

## Files

- cli/internal/agent/types.go
- cli/internal/agent/routing.go
- cli/internal/agent/catalog.go
- cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go (or wherever the bead prompt is assembled)","id":".execute-bead-wt-ddx-4817edfd-20260418T064927-78e29296-f5cec42a","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:routing"],"notes":"Superseded by agent-1a486c2e — single routing engine in agent/internal/routing","parent":"ddx-4817edfd","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"routing: context-window gating in evaluateCandidate","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:47.481306052Z"}

Update FEAT-006 (or wherever the execute-loop outcome contract is documented) to list the new structured outcomes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"ee729c4aed85c64e3f19a5f76d2addabd815918b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:16:41.722078707Z","description":"When ddx agent execute-bead lands a commit locally but merging into the base branch produces conflicts, the loop today preserves the result at refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<ts>-<base> and unclaims the bead with status: land_conflict. The bead reopens; subsequent attempts re-execute from scratch — burning more agent time and dollars even though the original work is intact in the preserved ref.

OBSERVED 2026-04-29 on ddx-52469547 (FEAT-015 bead 3, 'runtime fallback removal + audit-side migration'):
- Sonnet ran for ~3h, produced commit 12fbf6dd1b98 covering all 7 AC items.
- The base branch advanced significantly during the run (V1, V2, V3 vision beads + dogfood + concurrent work).
- The run hit the agent's 3h hard timeout right at the test phase.
- execute-loop preserved at refs/ddx/iterations/ddx-52469547/20260429T175646Z-aa7b31785a44 with status execution_failed (timeout, but functionally identical to land_conflict for the merge story).
- Operator attempted git merge --no-ff 12fbf6dd, hit 7 file-level conflicts (mechanical drift from parallel beads), aborted, and only avoided wasting the work because a parallel attempt (263b5eff) had landed the exact same scope cleanly. That parallel landing is a coincidence, not a guarantee.

The general case: a preserved iteration represents real money spent and real work done. land_conflict / execution_failed-with-result that bails to 'reopen and re-run' throws that work away. The loop should at least attempt to reuse the preserved result.

REQUESTED BEHAVIOR

1. On land_conflict (merge produces conflicts when applying the iteration onto current tip), execute-loop should:
   a. Try a 3-way merge of the preserved iteration ref onto current main using merge.conflictStyle=diff3 and ort strategy with -X ours/-X theirs heuristics. Only if the merge resolves cleanly under those heuristics, commit it as a non-fast-forward merge with a message like 'Merge preserved iteration ddx-<id> after base drift'.
   b. If 3-way auto-resolution fails, dispatch a SMALL focused agent run (cheap tier) with the conflict files, the preserved iteration's commit message, and the bead AC. Its sole job: resolve the conflicts, verify build/tests, commit. Do NOT re-do the bead from scratch.
   c. Only if (a) and (b) both fail, fall back to today's behavior (preserve, unclaim, set short cooldown, mark for human review with a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed — perhaps kind:land-conflict-unresolvable).

2. Cooldown for land_conflict-unresolvable should be short (5-30 min), not the 24h cap, since the conflict will resolve faster as soon as a human or another bead lands a clarifying change.

3. Same logic should apply to execution_failed-with-preserved-result (timeouts mid-merge): if a commit was produced before the failure, attempt to merge it before discarding the run.

4. Reuse the auto-recover plumbing landed for push race in ddx-a458af7c (landPushAutoRecover, capLoopCooldown). Specifically the conflict-vs-clean-recovery branching should mirror landPushAutoRecover's structure.

5. Regression tests:
   - Fake git that produces a mechanical conflict resolvable via -X ours: assert auto-resolution succeeds and bead lands.
   - Fake git that produces an irreconcilable structural conflict: assert escalation to focused-resolve agent (or, at minimum, that today's behavior is taken with the new structured outcome).
   - Preserved-iteration-on-timeout case: assert the preserved commit IS attempted as a merge before falling back.

ACCEPTANCE OF FAILURE: if the focused-resolve agent itself fails or escalates to BLOCKING, that's a structured outcome (kind:land-conflict-needs-human) — the bead is parked with the conflict context and preserved ref clearly recorded.

Related: ddx-a458af7c (push race auto-recover, this session's predecessor work).","events_attachment":"ddx-0097af14/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-0097af14","issue_type":"bug","labels":["execute-loop","beads","quality-of-life","area:agent"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:33:{"acceptance":"New file cli/internal/agent/script.go implements RunScript(opts) that reads a directive file, executes directives against opts.WorkDir using real filesystem and git operations, and returns a real Result. The harness is registered in cli/internal/agent/registry.go as `script` and marked always-available in Discover(). Directive grammar supports at minimum: append-line, create-file, delete-file, commit, set-exit, no-op, sleep-ms, fail-during. Environment variable interpolation supports ${DDX_BEAD_ID}, ${DDX_ATTEMPT_ID}, ${DDX_WORKER_ID}. Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/script_test.go cover: (1) append-line + commit produces a real commit with the expected content, (2) no-op produces zero commits and exit 0, (3) create-file + commit produces the file and a commit, (4) set-exit 1 makes the run return exit 1, (5) fail-during 2 executes directives 0 and 1 but errors on directive 2, (6) env var interpolation replaces ${DDX_BEAD_ID} with the value from Correlation, (7) invoking via `ddx agent run --harness=script --model=<file>` works end-to-end with a real temp git repo. go test ./internal/agent -run TestScript completes in under 5 seconds.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T14:49:12.379615428Z","description":"The execute-loop / execute-bead / land-coordinator system cannot be tested end-to-end today because every "fake" agent in the codebase returns a canned Result{} without actually writing files or making commits. `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go` is a prompt-response dictionary replayer — it returns canned stdout based on prompt hash but does not touch the filesystem or git. `fakeAgentRunner` and `fakeExecuteBeadGit` in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go` are in-memory mocks that track expected call counts. Neither produces actual commits that the orchestration layer can rebase, merge, or land.

The consequence: every test that claims to cover concurrency, serialization, or git orchestration uses mocked git and therefore cannot catch merge-race, rebase-conflict, or coordinator-serialization bugs. `TestConcurrentWorkersNoMergeRace` at execute_bead_orchestrator_test.go:228 is particularly misleading — it uses separate orchTestGitOps instances per worker so the shared-writer invariant is never exercised. The test passes because no shared state exists to race on.

## What this bead builds

A new deterministic `script` harness (cli/internal/agent/script.go, mirroring cli/internal/agent/virtual.go):

- Registered alongside claude/codex/agent/virtual in cli/internal/agent/registry.go
- Always available (no binary probe required)
- Takes a directive file (passed via RunOptions.Model or a new RunOptions.ScriptFile field — either works)
- Reads the directive file, executes each line against opts.WorkDir as real filesystem + git operations
- Returns a real Result{ExitCode, Output} that the loop and orchestrator can observe

## Directive grammar

Line-based, comments start with `#`. Each directive performs a real side effect:

```
append-line <path> <text>          # append text to file (create if missing)
create-file <path> <content>       # create or overwrite file
delete-file <path>                 # rm -f path
modify-line <path> <regex> <repl>  # sed-style in-place substitution
run <shell-command>                # exec in WorkDir with clean env
commit <message>                   # git add -A && git commit -m <message>
set-exit <code>                    # final exit code (default 0)
no-op                              # no commits, no files, exit 0
fail-during <index>                # synthetic failure at the Nth directive
sleep-ms <n>                       # deterministic delay (for scheduling tests)
```

Directives run in order. Environment variables DDX_BEAD_ID, DDX_ATTEMPT_ID, DDX_WORKER_ID are interpolated into text arguments so directives can be parameterized. Example:

```
append-line tests/smoke.txt "hello from ${DDX_BEAD_ID}"
commit "smoke: ${DDX_BEAD_ID}"
```

## Invocation

From the CLI: `ddx agent run --harness=script --model=./testdata/append-smoke.txt --prompt=/dev/null`

Programmatically from a test:
```go
runner := agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{Harness: "script"})
result, err := runner.Run(agent.RunOptions{
    WorkDir: wtPath,
    Model:   "testdata/append-smoke.txt",
    Correlation: map[string]string{"bead_id": beadID, "attempt_id": attemptID},
})
```

## Implementation notes

- Mirror the dispatch pattern in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:81 where `virtual` is intercepted
- Always-available in Discover() same as virtual + agent
- No external dependencies beyond what the agent package already uses (os, os/exec for git)
- Estimated size: ~200 lines of implementation + ~200 lines of tests
- Unit tests in cli/internal/agent/script_test.go must prove each directive actually performs its claimed side effect on a real temp git repo

## What this enables

Every test case in beads B (integration tests) and C (server runWorker regression test) depends on this harness existing. Without it, the land-coordinator redesign ships behind whatever real claude happens to do on the day the test runs.","id":"ddx-025046ac","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:testing","kind:infrastructure","phase:build"],"parent":"ddx-8746d8a6","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-53f5df0","status":"closed","title":"Implement deterministic script harness for orchestration stress testing","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:00:05.850093133Z"}

- **Inline context, not links.** Every bead description must be readable cold by an automated execute-bead worker that has no chat history and no open tabs.
- **Explicit in-scope file list.** Repo-relative paths. Bad: 'the auth module'. Good: 'cli/internal/auth/pipeline.go and cli/internal/auth/pipeline_test.go'.

- **Favor many small beads.** Rule of thumb: if a bead would touch more than ~3 files or more than one subsystem, decompose into child beads under an epic.
- **Acceptance is a command that passes, not a sentence.** 'X works' is not AC. 'cd cli && go test ./internal/foo/... passes and returns 0' is AC.
- **Implementations include tests.** The acceptance criteria must reference a test command.
- **Rollback/cleanup expectations** for partial changes — what 'leave it unclaimed' means in practice.
- **spec-id linkage** when a governing artifact exists (FEAT-*, SD-*, TD-*, ADR-*); omit when it doesn't.
- **CLI reference** at the end: ddx bead create/update/close/list/show/ready/blocked/dep/import/export/evidence.

Source material: existing /skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md (already contains much of this — especially the top-level "Execution-Ready" section added in an earlier refinement). Strip HELIX-specific rules: remove the mandatory 'helix' label and the phase:frame|design|test|build|deploy|iterate|review enumeration. Keep generic guidance ('label beads with area, kind, and any workflow tags your project uses').

In-scope files:
- skills/ddx/reference/beads.md (new)

Out-of-scope:
- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead ddx-bd852e86)

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:61:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d4874cfd75778a35969c330285e33a126e359108","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:21:53Z"}],"description":"## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).
- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.","efficacy_baseline":"recorded","events_attachment":"ddx-0a33bc5f/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent: provider error: provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-23T13:19:44Z","feature":"FEAT-019","id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-019","efficacy","perf"],"notes":"Efficacy now depends on ddx-2ceb02fa. This bead closes as 'investigation complete; see child beads for fixes'. ddx-b808df39 references this bead's findings in its implementation notes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:31:48Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"1d0b3e373a618623062909694332536c74f2630a","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.338860593Z","description":"## Context

ddx-b808df39 (P0: ddx agent stop/kill command for hung workers) treats hung-worker remediation as an operational need, but does NOT investigate the UNDERLYING cause of the hangs. The feature is a useful escape hatch, but shipping it without understanding WHY workers hang means we'll keep needing the escape hatch.

Opus fresh-eyes review (2026-04-18) flagged: 'Hung workers for 33h–142h are an operational defect independent of root cause' — the feature bead is justified, but a SIBLING root-cause investigation is missing from the portfolio.

## Observed

The referenced 33h and 142h hang durations in ddx-b808df39 imply multiple incidents where an execute-bead worker process remained in-progress past any reasonable upper bound for a single bead. Needs to investigate:

1. Is the worker process alive but stuck in a tool call (bash subprocess that never returns)?
2. Is it alive but mid-LLM-call with no timeout (provider hang + no client-side deadline)?
3. Is it dead but the bead's claimed_at never cleared (tracker-side leak)?
4. Is the worktree locked or fsync'd in a way that prevents cleanup?

## Fix direction

This bead is an INVESTIGATION, not a fix. Deliverable is a short report that:

1. Reproduces or observes at least one hang incident and captures:
   - ps / pgrep state of the worker process tree
   - stack traces via 'go tool pprof' against the worker (if alive)
   - Timeline from .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/*.jsonl (last event type + timestamp)
   - tracker claimed_at and retry_after state

2. Classifies the hang into one of the four categories above (or a new category).

3. Files concrete follow-up beads for each identified root cause:
   - Tool subprocess timeout if bash hangs
   - LLM client deadline if provider hangs
   - Claim expiry / heartbeat if tracker-side leak
   - File-lock / worktree cleanup if fs-side

4. Recommends whether ddx-b808df39's stop/kill mechanism is sufficient as mitigation or needs additional primitives.

## Relation to ddx-b808df39

ddx-b808df39 ships the OPERATOR TOOL to stop a hung worker. This bead finds the BUG that causes hangs. Both are needed; ship ddx-b808df39 first if the operator-tool is more urgent, then this investigation.

## Non-goals

- Fixing the root cause in this bead. Scope is investigation + follow-up bead filing.
- Preventing all possible hangs (impossible). Scope is the actually-observed hang modes.","events_attachment":"ddx-0a651925/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T04:50:48.095091931Z","id":"ddx-0a651925","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:investigation","area:agent","area:workers"],"notes":"Investigation complete (attempt 20260418T043148-1346e8a3). - RC2: idle-reset timer instead of wall-clock deadline for non-claude paths (executor.go:154, agent_runner.go:264).

- BashTool subprocess hang ruled out (120s default timeout). Tracker-side / fs-lock hypotheses are downstream of RC1.

- …-b0562380 P2 [RC3] bound compaction-stuck breaker by wall-clock
- …-34d039b7 P1 autonomous worker watchdog (complements ddx-b808df39)

ddx-b808df39's operator stop/kill is a correct mitigation but NOT sufficient as sole fix — until RC1 lands, operators will keep needing it. Ship ddx-b808df39 first, then RC1 → RC2 → RC4 → RC3 → watchdog.

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:66:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7d18cb4a228d9c3d6a8d3f381e34801b2cd7da2c","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:14:36.326164034Z","description":"'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.

## Observed

From CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:

  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json
  {
    "project_root": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx",  # WRONG
    "state": "running",
    "worker_id": "worker-20260415T011331-d0a4"
  }

I wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.

Currently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.

## Scope

- --project <path> or --project <name> flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use
- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar
- Default to CWD if unset and no flag
- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error
- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root

## Why this matters for the user's workflow

Without this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.

## Acceptance

- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run
- [ ] Env var fallback documented
- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection
- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission
- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting","events_attachment":"ddx-0b9c980a/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0b9c980a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop","tier:local"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:05:45.856928108Z"}

Existing ddx agent log behavior (no --bead flag) is unchanged. ddx agent log <session-id> single-session detail view is unchanged.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T10:13:22Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"4d938dd00e470417cb39fc139c564bc3d74bd6a4","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:35:04.508596568Z","description":"ddx agent log is fully implemented (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go lines 745-807) and reads .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl. Each SessionEntry has a Correlation map (cli/internal/agent/types.go) that includes bead_id when the session was created by execute-bead. However there is no way to filter sessions by bead — you must know the session ID in advance or scroll through all recent sessions.

This bead adds --bead <id> filtering and a per-bead attempt summary view to the existing command.

## Required changes

### 1. --bead flag on ddx agent log (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)
When --bead <bead-id> is provided:
  - Scan all SessionEntry records in sessions.jsonl
  - Filter to entries where Correlation["bead_id"] == bead-id
  - Sort ascending by Timestamp (attempt order)
  - Display in attempt-history table format (see below)

No change to the default behavior (no --bead = show recent N sessions as today).

### 2. Attempt history table columns
ATTEMPT | STARTED | DURATION | HARNESS | MODEL | OUTCOME | TOKENS | COST | SESSION
- ATTEMPT: 1-based index in chronological order
- OUTCOME: merged / preserved / no-changes / error (from ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome via Correlation or result.json)
- SESSION: truncated session ID (8 chars) for use with ddx agent log <session-id>
- COST: formatted as /bin/zsh.0042 or 'local' if cost_usd == 0

### 3. Outcome resolution
SessionEntry does not directly store outcome — it stores exit_code and error. The full outcome is in .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/result.json (ExecuteBeadResult struct). When rendering the attempt table:
  - Use Correlation["attempt_id"] to find .ddx/executions/{attempt_id}/result.json
  - Read ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome from that file
  - Fall back to exit_code == 0 ? 'success' : 'error' if result.json is absent

### 4. Summary line
After the table, print a one-line summary:
  '<bead-id>: N attempts, M merged, K preserved, J errors. Last attempt: <elapsed> ago.'

### 5. --json passthrough
When --bead and --json are both set, emit the filtered SessionEntry array as JSON with an added outcome field resolved from result.json.

## Key files
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — newAgentLogCommand() lines 745-807, add --bead flag and filter path

Fix execute-bead/execute-loop result classification so no-change attempts cannot be treated as successful landed work.

## Scope
- Reproduce the no-change-success path from execute-loop
- Ensure attempts with no commits / no diff / unchanged result_rev cannot produce `status=success` or close the bead by default
- Align loop close behavior with the result classification contract so only true successful work closes a bead
- Add regression coverage around no-change, preserved, and successful landed cases

## Boundaries
- Do not change the documented merge-by-default policy for real successful executions
- Keep this fix focused on outcome/close classification, not broader prompt-quality or routing issues","id":"ddx-1c74c9ee","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:cli","area:bead"],"notes":"Fixed in a2db669: no-change execute-bead attempts are no longer treated as success or bead-closing outcomes.","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Do not classify no-change execute-loop attempts as success","updated_at":"2026-04-10T23:19:09.20387414Z"}

`ddx agent execute-bead` prompt synthesis failed to resolve a live path-style `spec-id` into governing references, so the generated execution prompt told the agent there were no governing references even though the referenced file exists in the execution snapshot.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, inspect bead `helix-fef22846`; it carries `spec-id=workflows/README.md`.
2. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` or `ddx agent execute-bead helix-fef22846 --json`.
3. Open `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/prompt.md`.
4. Observe the prompt includes `- spec-id: workflows/README.md` but the `## Governing References` section says `No governing references were resolved from the current execution snapshot.`

## Observed gap
`executeBeadResolveGoverningRefs()` builds the doc graph and looks up only by graph document ID. Path-like `spec-id` values that point to real files in the repo are dropped instead of being resolved as governing references.

## Why this matters
This is exactly the context the execution prompt tells the agent to read before changing code or docs. When DDx drops an existing repo-relative path reference, agent execution becomes less grounded and the artifact bundle misstates the available governing context.","events_attachment":"ddx-1f434484/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1f434484","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:docs","area:cli"],"priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Resolve path-style spec-id values in execute-bead governing references","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:12:11.237799965Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:137:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T01:13:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:17.497753676Z","description":"The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.

## Observed

Running 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):

1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.

2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:

    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    {"type":"compaction.start",...}
    {"type":"compaction.end",...,"data":{"messages_after":249,"messages_before":249,"no_compaction":true,"success":false}}
    ...

3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.

4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.

## Expected

- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate
- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after < messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely
- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin

## Scope

- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status
- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn
- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls
- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget

## Evidence

- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)
- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master
- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally
- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic
- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome
- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","events_attachment":"ddx-25a41f51/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T01:39:31.55993889Z","id":"ddx-25a41f51","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-e80e780a","status":"closed","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.206978601Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:160:{"acceptance":"AC1. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[provider].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per provider.

AC2. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-tier via the model-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking model (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat models keep the 15-min default.

AC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.

AC4. When ProviderTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.<name>.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').

AC5. execute-loop / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.

AC6. Default for known-thinking-model surfaces (provider.type=lmstudio AND model name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T20:58:18Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"932496","closing_commit_sha":"99375eaf330ea13838d40d2176d193ae3cd3453b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:50:46.475563514Z","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30
  const DefaultProviderRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute

This is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every provider regardless of model class. It is set on every service path:
  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout
  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout

The comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'

But that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:
  const DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute

The 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.

Concrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):

1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)
   - status: no_changes
   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)
   - log: 'WARN provider error, retrying attempt=1 err="provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s" delay=1s'
   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.

2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)
   - All 3 hit 'provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt
   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each

Why thinking models hit this:
qwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning models can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the harness considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the harness is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.

Why arbitrary timeouts are not helping:

a) Same value for cloud providers (claude, codex) and local providers (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking models routinely need >15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.

b) No way to opt out of the cap per provider, per model, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.

c) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.

d) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking model the timeout doesn't mean 'broken provider', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","events_attachment":"ddx-2c63bb95/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-29T21:15:18.844737462Z","id":"ddx-2c63bb95","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:harness","kind:design-flaw"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-2db5279a","status":"closed","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultProviderRequestTimeout kills thinking-model requests; not configurable","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:16:13.255743379Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:161:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.

2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.

3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.

4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.

5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.

6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.

7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.

8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner "No sessions recorded between X and Y" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.

9. **Playwright e2e.**
   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.
   - Runs the migration command.
   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.
   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d733f7f20828708c5179f0ceff9462eaa34654eb","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:22:41.990684692Z","description":"## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.","events_attachment":"ddx-2ceb02fa/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model "minimax/minimax-m2.7" for harness "gemini"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-010","id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-006","perf","observability"],"notes":"## 2026-04-22 — User-directed amendment: shard the index

Per user feedback: a single `sessions.index.jsonl` just replays the same problem at smaller scale. The aggregate needs to be split.

### Revised storage shape

- Directory: `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/` (per-project, under each registered project's `.ddx`).
- Inside: **monthly shards** named `sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`.
- Each shard is pointer-only, same fields as the previously-proposed index (id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath, nativeLogRef).
- Writer resolves the target shard from `time.Now().UTC()` at append time and creates the shard file lazily on first write of the month.
- Heavy bodies (prompt, response, stderr) stay where they already live — `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` and agent-log streams — and are lazy-loaded by the `session(id:)` resolver.

### Why monthly, not daily / size-based / SQLite

- **Daily** — produces tens of thousands of files over the project lifetime; ReadDir becomes the cost.
- **Size-based** — requires coordinating rotation with the append; race-prone and hard to test.
- **SQLite** — overkill for first pass; revisit if a single monthly shard grows past tens of MB.
- **Monthly** — ≤12 shards/year, human-readable filenames, date-range queries map trivially to shard selection, bounded shard size in practice.

### Reader behavior

- Default Sessions query loads the **current + previous shard** (covers "recent activity" for any current date).
- Date-range queries map the range to a shard set and read only those.
- Pagination beyond the default window triggers older-shard loads on demand.
- No shard-coordination lock needed: each month's file is append-only and only the current-month shard is actively written.

### Legacy migration

- `ddx agent log reindex` reads the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` once and splits by each entry's `timestamp` field into the appropriate monthly shards under `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/`.
- After migration, the legacy file is renamed `sessions.jsonl.legacy` (not deleted) and never read again by the server. Hand-removal is the user's call.
- Idempotent: re-running the command merges without duplicating (index row dedup by `id`).

### Revised acceptance criteria (supersedes criteria 1, 5, 6, 7 on original description)

1. **Sharded index writer.** Single shared call site (from `service.Execute` or equivalent wrapper) appends one line to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, computing the shard from `time.Now().UTC()`. Test: write across a simulated month boundary and assert two shards exist with the correct split.

5. **Legacy migration shards too.** `ddx agent log reindex` splits `sessions.jsonl` into monthly shards by entry timestamp. Integration test uses a fixture with entries across 3 distinct months and asserts 3 shards are produced with correct contents and no duplicates on re-run.

6. **Server reads the shard directory.** `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` reads `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/*.jsonl`, respects any date-range arg, and loads only the shards that intersect the range. Unit test seeds 6 monthly shards and asserts a 2-month query reads exactly 2 files.

7. **Perf on sharded index.** Default Sessions view (current + previous shard) resolves in ≤50ms p95 on a fixture with 12 monthly shards × 10k entries each (120k total). Capture baseline (single-file read of same corpus) vs sharded in bead notes.

(Criteria 2, 3, 4, 8, 9 from the original acceptance list remain as written.)

### Scope clarifier

This amendment does not change the single-call-site writer guarantee or the lazy-body resolver — those remain the load-bearing ideas. The amendment is specifically about **how the pointer index is physically stored**.
## 2026-04-23 — Implementation benchmark and evidence

Baseline: `go test ./internal/agent -run '^$' -bench 'Benchmark(ReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow|LegacySessionsJSONLFullScan)$' -benchtime=1x` measured legacy single-file full scan/sort of 120k legacy-shaped rows with 2KB embedded responses at `BenchmarkLegacySessionsJSONLFullScan-12 1 1731705957 ns/op`.

After fix: the same command measured the sharded default-window reader at `BenchmarkReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow-12 1 8103661 ns/op` for 12 monthly shards x 10k rows each (120k total), with the default query reading only the current + previous shard window. Environment footer: linux/arm64, Go go1.26.2.

Acceptance evidence: `TestSessionIndexWritesMonthlyShards`, `TestSessionIndexShardFilesSelectsOnlyIntersectingDateRange`, `TestReindexLegacySessionsSplitsShardsAndDedupes`, `TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow`, `TestProductionAgentExecutionPathsUseIndexedServiceWriter`, `TestGraphQLAgentSessions`, `TestAgentLogReindexMigratesLegacyFile`, existing agent usage/status/processmetrics tests, and `e2e/sessions.spec.ts` cover writer sharding, bounded reads, migration, lazy body loading, deprecated-file read removal, UI gap banner, and live refresh.

REVIEW:BLOCK

Missing GraphQL changes: no update to `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go` for index-backed `sessions` or lazy `session(id:)` body loading.
Missing migration command: no `ddx agent log reindex` or equivalent CLI implementation and no fixture-based migration test.
Missing CLI usage/status updates: no changes to `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` or `cli/cmd/status.go` to stop reading legacy `sessions.jsonl`.
Missing performance validation: no benchmark or fixture proving 10,000 index entries resolve within the required threshold.
Missing UI gap/banner behavior and Playwright coverage for `/sessions`, lazy loading, migration, and live feed updates.

REVIEW:BLOCK

**AC 1–3**: No `sessions.index.jsonl` schema, writer in `service.Execute`, or updated GraphQL resolver in `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go`.
**AC 4**: No `session(id: ID!)` resolver for lazy-loading prompt/response/stderr from execution bundles.
**AC 5**: No `ddx agent log reindex` command or migration logic.

**AC 7**: No benchmark captured.
**AC 8–9**: No frontend changes or Playwright tests.

Missing Playwright e2e covering migration, latest timestamp visibility, lazy body load, and live feed updates.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-b9f50300","status":"closed","title":"Sessions UI is blind to post-2026-04-19 activity: service.Execute path no longer writes sessions.jsonl","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:18:24.247572547Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:163:{"acceptance":"All three child beads are closed. The exit-gate one-liner above runs end-to-end against a real ready bead on a host with a healthy vidar-omlx provider and produces a non-error closing status (success / already_satisfied) or a meaningful provider-side error — not 'unexpected end of JSON input' and not 'Model not found' 404s for catalog-missing refs.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"cf4544f0cb71adcbe7ab7e10fec340474756704f","created_at":"2026-04-18T00:57:02.418274493Z","description":"## Target user experience

An operator declares a local MLX server (omlx flavor) once in .agent/config.yaml:

    providers:
      vidar-omlx:
        type: openai-compat
        base_url: http://vidar:1235/v1
        flavor: omlx

And invokes work with a canonical model ref:

    ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6

Expected outcome: DDx fuzzy-matches qwen/qwen3.6 against vidar-omlx's discovered models (finds Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit), dispatches through the embedded agent runtime, streams a valid response, runs the execute-bead worktree loop, and lands or preserves the attempt with an honest status.

Today this fails at multiple layers. The audit for this epic (2026-04-17) traced the resolution path end-to-end and identified concrete structural gaps between SD-015/SD-023 and the current implementation. A fresh-eyes review (codex + opus, 2026-04-17) pressure-tested the plan and led to the child-bead list below.

## Child gaps (all must land for the target UX)

**Foundation (unblocks everything):**
- ddx-8610020e (P1, bug): RouteRequest has no Provider field; --provider is silently dropped at routing normalization unless it names a harness. AC includes explicit --harness × --provider precedence rules.

**Discovery layer:**
- ddx-0486e601 (P1, bug): fuzzy matcher needs canonical-prefix stripping + case-fold AND per-provider-aware pool variant. Merged from two earlier beads (normalization + per-provider) per fresh-eyes review — same file, same test surface, one coherent change.

**Integration layer:**
- ddx-2f5a2284 (P2, bug): catalog/Profile paths skip discovery; fuzzy only fires for uncataloged pins. Fix adds discovery consultation when req.Provider is pinned. AC includes bare-leaf refs ('qwen3.6' not just 'qwen/qwen3.6').
- ddx-3c5ba7cc (P1, task): tier escalation respects --provider affinity.

**Dispatch layer:**
- ddx-6a5dfe35 (P1, bug): parse failure at ddx-agent ↔ omlx boundary. Re-scoped per fresh-eyes review to require wire-level evidence before blaming upstream — the 'direct curl works' isolation is insufficient to pin the defect.

**Documentation:**
- ddx-e3493010 (P2, task): 'Resolution Path' developer guide tracing CLI flag -> HTTP dispatch. AC includes CI grep verifying file:line citations resolve (prevents doc rot).

**Phase 2 — tracked as siblings, NOT exit-gate blockers:**
- ddx-4817edfd (P2, task): capability gating (context window, tool support, effort, permissions). Tracks sub-bead decomposition; epic's exit gate does NOT require it.
- ddx-0216b966 (P3, task): fuzzy match tiebreak — prefer latest version over shortest-suffix. Inherited quirk; flag + track.

**Closed as invalid or merged:**
- ddx-e59e7fe8 (catalog entries): wrong diagnosis (closed as invalid). Fuzzy normalization + per-provider pool + discovery integration make catalog entries unnecessary.
- ddx-b388f45a (per-provider fuzzy): merged into ddx-0486e601 per review — same file, same test surface.

## Evidence

- .ddx/executions/20260418T005017-ac81ac97/ — attempt 1 (tier escalation + catalog mismatch)
- .ddx/executions/20260418T005052-3aad2cfa/ — attempt 2 (parse failure)
- Audit file:line citations in bead bodies; gap map in this description.

## Exit gate

'ddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6' against a real ready bead on a host with healthy vidar-omlx completes with a closing status (success / already_satisfied) or a meaningful provider/model error — not 'unexpected end of JSON input' and not 'Model not found' 404s for refs the discovery layer could have fuzzy-matched.
