.ddx/executions/20260416T044613-0f04e8ce/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).",

## Process

1. **Read first.** Before writing any code, read the files the bead description names. If the description says "A1 landed X at <path>", read that path. If it references a spec section, read that section. Do NOT start editing without seeing the existing code — this is how you avoid making the same change twice or breaking an invariant you did not know about.

2. **Plan the work briefly.** Internally note which files you will touch, which tests you will add, and which verification commands you will run. Keep the plan short — one or two sentences.

3. **Implement.** Use `edit` for targeted changes to existing files and `write` for brand-new files. Use `read` (not `bash: cat`) to read files. Use `grep` (not `bash: rg`) to search. Use `ls` (not `bash: ls`) for directory listings.

4. **Verify before committing.** Run the project's test and lint commands — typically `cargo test -p <crate>` and `cargo clippy -p <crate> -- -D warnings` for Rust, `bun test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description will usually name the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If anything fails, diagnose and fix it before committing.

5. **Commit exactly once.** Use `git add <specific-paths>` with the file paths you actually changed — never `git add -A`, which can pick up unrelated files in the worktree. Use a conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Commit implementation and tests in the same commit so the reviewer sees them together.

6. **Stop after the commit succeeds.** Return immediately. Do not continue reading files, running extra tests, or asking yourself follow-up questions — the work is done, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success to the orchestrator. Continuing past the commit risks runaway loops.

## If you cannot finish

.ddx/executions/20260416T044736-e5405c7f/prompt.md:5:## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).

.ddx/executions/20260416T044736-e5405c7f/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).",
